See earlier disinformation cases alleging that Ukraine "sold itself to external control, "is a Western puppet that creates problems for Russia, that the Russophobic Baltic States are beggars and vassals of the West, that the main activity of the Latvian state is the trade of Russophobia, and that the Baltic states are dying because they chose not to be with Russia.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The Russian Federation is not looking for military conflicts with its neighbours; it does not threaten anyone.
label: russia_narrative

text: A recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about peaceful Russia, casting it as a benign, non-aggressive state. Russia has a long record of violating other states' sovereignty and territorial integrity. Since 1991, Russia has been involved in several international conflicts, including interventions in neighbouring states, such as Georgia in 2008 and Ukraine in 2014. Many international organisations condemned the Russian occupation of Abkhazia and South Ossetia, see e.g. the OSCE statements here and here and the European Parliament's declaration. It was also Russia that provoked the war in Ukraine. The Office of the Prosecutor of the International Criminal Court has stated that “the information available suggests that the situation within the territory of Crimea and Sevastopol amounts to an international armed conflict between Ukraine and the Russian Federation. This international armed conflict began at the latest on 26 February 2014 when the Russian Federation deployed members of its armed forces to gain control over parts of the Ukrainian territory without the consent of the Ukrainian Government. The European Union stated in July 2014 that "arms and fighters continue flowing into Ukraine from the Russian Federation". Russian President Vladimir Putin admitted to the military presence of Russia in Ukraine in 2015. In 2014 Russia also annexed a part of Ukrainian territory - Crimean peninsula. The so-called referendum on the peninsula was organised in a matter of weeks by a self-proclaimed Crimean leadership lacking democratic legitimacy and installed by armed Russian military personnel following the seizure of public buildings. The EU does not recognise it. The United Nations General Assembly Resolution No. 68/262, adopted on 27 March 2014 and supported by 100 states, clearly says that Russia's actions in Crimea, as well as the referendum held in the Autonomous Republic of Crimea, violate international law.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The intrusion of the British destroyer Defender into Russian territorial waters had been a complex provocation and had been carried out not only by the UK but also by the USA.
label: russia_narrative

text: Repeated disinformation narrative about the presence of the UK Navy in the Black Sea and a recurring disinformation narrative about the status of Crimea, aimed at justifying Russia's occupation of the peninsula from Ukraine seven years ago and depicting NATO as an aggressor against Kremlin-controlled territory. On 23 June, the UK Ministry of Defence Press Office mentioned that "the Royal Navy ship [was] conducting innocent passage through Ukrainian territorial waters in accordance with international law." UK Secretary of State for Defence added that the ship "carried out a routine transit from Odesa towards Georgia across the Black Sea" through internationally recognised Ukrainian territorial waters. Additionally, the fact that the passage of HMS Defender from a maritime area that is still internationally recognised as Ukrainian territorial waters is somehow connected to NATO's exercises so as to provoke Russian aggression is simply not valid. NATO is a defensive alliance, whose purpose is to protect its member states. During the Brussels Summit Communiqué of 14 June 2021, NATO has made it clear that:"NATO does not seek confrontation and poses no threat to Russia. Decisions we have taken are fully consistent with our international commitments, and therefore cannot be regarded by anyone as contradicting the NATO-Russia Founding Act". The Alliance announces its military exercises well in advance and they are subject to international observation. NATO strongly supports efforts to build confidence and transparency in military activities and exercises. Exercises like Sea Breeze and Defender Europe 2021 are purely defensive in nature and are organised for NATO Allies and partners to strengthen and fortify the level of interoperability. Similar messages also imply that NATO-Ukraine joint war games threaten Russia's security and serve as a justification of its military build-up along Ukraine’s border. Russia occupied Crimea in March 2014. No international body has recognised the so-called referendum in Crimea, not the UN, nor the EU or the UK. UNGA Resolution 68/262, which condemned Russia's illegal actions in Ukraine, was supported by 100 United Nations member states. Only 10 states opposed it. See more cases related to Crimea here.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Once "Sputnik V" is accepted into the list of approved vaccines in the European Union, this would mean a diplomatic victory for Russia, after trade with the (European) Union was disrupted for years, due to EU sanctions after it (Russia) reclaimed Crimea.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the illegal annexation of Crimea and the sanctions against Russia. Since March 2014, the EU has progressively imposed restrictive measures against Russia which were prolonged until 31 July 2021. The measures were adopted in response to the illegal annexation of Crimea, the deliberate destabilisation of Ukraine and the poisoning of opposition leader Alexei Navalny. There was no such thing as a “reclamation” but in effect an illegal annexation of the Ukrainian territory. No international body recognises the so-called referendum in Crimea. The UN announced its non-recognition of the illegal annexation, and the EU continues to strongly condemn this violation of international law. While the level of trade between the EU and Russia has seen some fluctuations in recent years, a complete halt of trades between both sides has never seen light. It was not, and is not, the purpose of the sanctions to bring the trade to a halt. However, Russia's own decisions to ban imports from the EU of primarily food products have reduced the trade volumes leading to higher prices for ordinary Russians. In 2020, Russia is the EU's fifth-largest trading partner and the EU is Russia's largest trading partner, with two-way trade in goods value of €232 billion in 2019. The three largest importers from Russia in the EU were Germany (EUR 19 554 million), the Netherlands (EUR 13 417 million) and Poland (EUR 9 964 million). Finland (34.2 %) had the highest share for Russia in its extra-EU imports. While three largest exporters to Russia in the EU were Germany (EUR 23 155 million), Poland (EUR 7 136 million) and Italy (EUR 7 101 million). Latvia (34.3 %) had the highest share for Russia in its extra-EU exports. See related pro-Kremlin disinformation claims in our database alleging that EU sanctions on Russia are a "circus" and a "fake story"; or that the EU lost more from sanctions than Russia; or that Russophobia weakened Europe; or that sanctions actually help Russia; or that Crimea was reunited with homeland Russia; or that Moscow did not violate international law when reunifying Crimea with Russia.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: It is quite obvious that Crimea is a part of Russia, and no one will give it to anyone. Moreover, the peninsula was reunited with Russia based on legal procedures established in international law. And Russia has enough strength to defend its territory.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the illegal annexation of Crimea in 2014. No international body recognises the so-called referendum in Crimea. The UN General Assembly adopted a resolution entitled “Territorial integrity of Ukraine”, stating that the referendum in Crimea was not valid and could not serve as a basis for any change in the status of the peninsula. On 17 December 2018, the UN General Assembly confirmed its non-recognition of the illegal annexation of Crimea. The Council on Foreign Relations lists one key legal reason for the illegitimacy of the Crimean referendum: Changes to the territory of Ukraine must be approved by a referendum of all Ukrainian people. The Cambridge International Law Journal states that there are a variety of reasons when secession might be legal: (1) if the people concerned are subject to decolonization; (2) if it is envisaged by the legislation of the parent state; (3) if a territory inhabited by a certain people is occupied or annexed after 1945; (4) the secessionist constitute a "people"; (5) the parent state flagrantly violate human rights; (6) no other effective remedies under national or international law may exist if any of the above-mentioned conditions are met. However, in the case of Ukraine and Crimea, none of these conditions were met. Vladimir Putin, the President of Russia, recognised on 17 April 2015 that "our soldiers were deployed in Crimea to help the inhabitants express their opinion." Vladimir Putin admitted that the plan to annex Crimea was ordered weeks before even the Maidan protests culminated. Thus, the previous claim by Putin and Russia that Russia did not control the "little Green Men" and later that they responded to human rights violations etc. proved to be false. The European Union does not recognise and continues to strongly condemn this violation of international law, which remains a challenge to the international security order. In response to the illegal annexation of Crimea, the EU has imposed restrictive measures against the Russian Federation. On 21 June 2021, the EU renewed sanctions introduced in response to the illegal annexation of Crimea and Sevastopol by the Russian Federation for a further year until 23 June 2022. See related disinformation claims alleging that Crimea has never been Ukraine; that Crimea rejoined Russia following a referendum; that Crimea "sailed" to Russia forever and the West agrees with it and that The issue of Crimea is closed once and for all. Read more about the false narrative "Crimea was always Russian" here.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The HMS Defender’s entry into Russian territorial waters was a violation of Russian territorial integrity and a deliberate provocation by the United Kingdom and the United States.
label: russia_narrative

text: A claim that the HMS Defender violated Russian territorial waters, and that it consequently breached maritime law rests on an assumption that Russian illegal annexation of Crimea was in fact in accordance with international law. However, the annexation of Crimean Peninsula lacks recognition by both Ukraine and international community as a whole. Moreover, since HMS Defender’s passage was continuous and expeditious, and since there is no indication that HMS Defender ever used a threat of force or any similar action, HMS Defender conducted innocent passage in full accordance with United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea to which both UK and Russia are parties. This disinformation narrative is closely related to various accusations of Western provocations against Russia, while it also relies on pro-Kremlin narratives regarding the lawful status of Crimea as part of the Russian federation. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that UK violated the 19882 Law of the Seas Treaty, or that an entry of HMS Defender was a deliberate act.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: UK military vessel HMS Defender crossed illegally through the territorial waters of Russia off the Crimea peninsula. This was a provocation and a sign that the United States and the United Kingdom want to appear friendly to Ukraine.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring disinformation narrative about the presence of the UK Navy in the Black Sea and a recurring disinformation narrative about the status of Crimea, aimed at justifying Russia's occupation of the peninsula from Ukraine and presenting NATO as an aggressor. On 23 June, the UK Ministry of Defence Press Office mentioned that "the Royal Navy ship [was] conducting innocent passage through Ukrainian territorial waters in accordance with international law." UK Secretary of State for Defence added that the ship "carried out a routine transit from Odesa towards Georgia across the Black Sea" through internationally recognised Ukrainian territorial waters. Russia occupied Crimea in March 2014. No international body has recognised the so-called referendum in Crimea, not the UN, nor the EU or the UK. UNGA Resolution 68/262, which condemned Russia's illegal actions in Ukraine, was supported by 100 United Nations member states. Only 10 states opposed it. See more cases related to Crimea here.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Unfortunately, members of the Parliamentary Assembly of the Organisation for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE) have no information, they lack basic education. Most of them do not even know that Ukraine is historically a part of Russia; they believe that it is a separate country that was oppressed by an empire.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin media denying Ukrainian statehood.In reality, the territory of present-day Ukraine had been divided between the Russian Empire and the Austrian-Hungarian Empire for centuries. Many new countries appeared in Europe in the wake of Russia’s Bolshevik revolution in 1917 and World War One (1914-1918), the Ukrainian People’s republic (aka the Ukrainian National Republic) being one of these. It was later occupied by Communist Russia and forcibly incorporated into the Soviet Union. Ukraine assumed its internationally recognised borders in 1991 when the USSR broke down. It is true that Ukrainians and Russians share a common history, have similar languages and are mostly Orthodox Christian believers. Many neighbouring European nations have a common history, linguistic similarity and religious affinity as well. Although some of them were parts of other, bigger states at some point in history, all modern European nations are now equal and independent states, and none of them claims their neighbours’ territory. Ukraine has elected six presidents since it regained independence. Despite Moscow’s fierce opposition, Kyiv set out on a pro-Western course when Petro Poroshenko was elected president in 2014. Kyiv signed the Association Agreement with the European Union in the same year, and constitutional amendments were introduced in 2019, which set Ukraine’s membership of NATO and EU as a strategic goal. The incumbent, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, reaffirmed his predecessor’s policy. The illegally annexed peninsula of Crimea, which Russia considers its own, was part of the Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic at the time of disintegration of the USSR. Therefore, it belongs to Ukraine, which was recognised both internationally and by the Treaty on Friendship, Cooperation, and Partnership between Ukraine and the Russian Federation of 1997 and the Treaty between Ukraine and the Russian Federation on the Ukrainian-Russian state border of 2003. The European Union, other leading democracies, and international organisations regard Crimea as part of Ukraine. They do not recognise its illegal annexation and call for the restoration of the territorial integrity of Ukraine. As for the education of the members of the OSCE Parliamentary Assembly, many of them have a sound academic background and know that Ukraine does exist as an independent state. It is common knowledge that Ukraine, Russia and all other ex-Soviet republics gained independence in 1991 after the disintegration of the USSR. If members of the Russian delegation deny this fact, they seem to lack either basic education or common sense. Regardless of the educational level of the Parliamentary Assembly's members and their views on world history, it is essential that Russia should keep in line with international law and respect its neighbours and partners. Read more disinformation cases about Ukrainian statehood.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: On June 23, the British destroyer Defender entered the territorial sea for three kilometers and crossed the border near Cape Fiolent in the Crimea. The Russian border ship fired a warning and the Su-24M aircraft bombed along the path of the destroyer.The British Ministry of Defence and the government office said the naval ship "made a peaceful passage through the territorial waters of Ukraine and no one fired at it", while Russia was conducting "exercises."However, a BBC journalist on board the destroyer confirmed that the ship had deliberately crossed the border.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a distortion of the report of the BBC journalist aboard the British destroyer HMS Defender, defence correspondent Jonathan Beale, as part of a disinformation campaign about the incident near Crimea’s coast on 23 June 2021. As can be seen in his original report, he uttered the term “deliberate” in a different context to the one portrayed here.His exact words were: “HMS Defender is going to sail within what Russia now claims as its territorial waters, but following a legally recognised shipping lane”. In a written addendum to the story on the BBC website, Beale stated:“This would be a deliberate move to make a point to Russia. HMS Defender was going to sail within the 12 mile (19km) limit of Crimea's territorial waters. The captain insisted he was only seeking safe passage through an internationally recognised shipping lane.”In this article, his statements were merged and the nuances are purposefully ignored, presenting as if he allegedly confirms a deliberate border violation in order to present the incident as a British provocation, in line with the statements of Russian top officials. Given that Russia’s annexation of Crimea is internationally considered as illegal, the affirmation that the vessel “entered Russian territorial waters in the Black Sea” is also false. See other examples of disinformation about the HMS Defender incident, such as the claim that UK destroyer’s passage near Crimea violated international law, that its presence in the Black Sea was a NATO provocation or that the UK wanted Russia to shoot at the vessel.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Washington and London play along with Kyiv even in situations that could cause conflicts. It is about disposition in the Black Sea. The border violation with the excuse that this territory does not belong to Russian Federation is a provocation. Moscow is ready to fight back.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring and continuing disinformation narrative about the passage of HMS Defender through waters near Crimea and in general connected to the narrative about the status of Crimea. It is not true that the UK and US provoked Russia with the transit of the British ship HMS Defender through the Black Sea towards Georgia. There is no evidence to support the claim that this was a planned provocation. The Black Sea transit of the HMS Defender on the Odesa to Georgia route is within full legal maritime law guaranteed as the “right of innocent passage” by the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), to which both the UK and Russia are parties. The allegation that the HMS violated Russian territorial waters is a manipulation of the issue of Crimea. The HMS Defender transited 12 miles offshore in the waters of Crimea, a territory militarily occupied and forcibly annexed by Russia in 2014 in a referendum that was not recognised by the UN, nor the EU or the UK or any international body. There is also no evidence that Washington or London are planning any kind of “new provocations” against Russia or seeking conflict with Moscow. See other similar narratives: BBC reporter aboard HMS Defender revealed incident in Russian waters was a deliberate act; HMS Defender’s violation of Russian territorial waters was a provocation by the UK and the US; the UK flagrantly violated Russia’s maritime border.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The HMS Defender’s entry into Russian territorial waters was a violation of Russian territorial integrity and a deliberate provocation by the United Kingdom and the United States.
label: russia_narrative

text: A claim that the HMS defender violated Russian territorial waters, and that it consequently breached maritime law rests on an assumption that Russian illegal annexation of Crimea was in fact in accordance with international law. However, the annexation of the Crimean Peninsula lacks recognition by both Ukraine and the international community as a whole. Moreover, since HMS Defender’s passage was continuous and expeditious, and since there is no indication that HMS Defender ever used a threat of force or any similar action, Defender conducted innocent passage in full accordance with the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea to which both UK and Russia are members. This disinformation narrative is closely related to various accusations of western provocations against Russia, while it also relies on pro-Russian narratives regarding the lawful status of Crimea as part of the Russian federation. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that the UK violated the 1982 Law of the Seas Treaty, or that an entry of HMS Defender was a deliberate act.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: A BBC journalist made a call aboard the British ship that entered Russian territorial waters in the Black Sea on Wednesday and confirmed the warnings of the Russian patrol boats amid the noise generated by the Russian planes flying over the British ship. The reporter revealed that their transit was a "deliberate act of following a recognised international shipping route" from Ukraine to Georgia.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a distortion of the report of the BBC journalist aboard the British destroyer HMS Defender, defence correspondent Jonathan Beale, as part of a disinformation campaign about the incident near Crimea’s coasts on 23 June 2021. As can be seen in his original reporting, he uttered the term “deliberate” in a different context to the one portrayed here. His exact words were: “HMS Defender is going to sail within what Russia now claims as its territorial waters, but following a legally recognised shipping lane”. In a written addendum to the story on the BBC website, Beale stated: “This would be a deliberate move to make a point to Russia”. In this disinformation piece, these two sentences are merged and the nuances are purposefully ignored in order to present the incident as a British provocation, in line with the statements of Russian top officials. Given that Russia’s annexation of Crimea is internationally considered as illegal, the affirmation that the vessel “entered Russian territorial waters in the Black Sea” is also false. See other examples of disinformation about the HMS Defender incident, such as the claim that UK destroyer’s passage near Crimea violated international law, that its presence in the Black Sea was a NATO provocation or that the UK wanted Russia to shoot at the vessel.

text: After a brief silence caused by the meeting between Vladimir Putin and Joe Biden, the West started to talk about new sanctions against Russia again. Recently, these talks have become a routine phenomenon unrelated to specific actions by Moscow.
label: russia_narrative

text: A recurrent disinformation narrative about Western sanctions against Russia, falsely portraying them as ungrounded and prejudiced. The EU introduced a series of restrictive measures against Russia starting in 2014 as a response to the illegal annexation of Crimea and actions undermining or threatening the territorial integrity, sovereignty and independence of Ukraine. Sanctions were also imposed on Russian individuals over serious human rights violations, and the use of chemical weapons in the assassination attempt of Alexei Navalny. For an overview of sanctions imposed by the US over Russia's 2014 invasion of Ukraine, malicious cyber activities and influence operations (including election interference), human rights abuses, use of a chemical weapon, weapons proliferation, illicit trade with North Korea, support to the governments of Syria and Venezuela, and use of energy exports as a coercive or political tool, see here. Sanctions can be amended, extended, lifted or waived by both the US and the EU. Both the EU and the US have emphasised repeatedly that the sanctions imposed on Russia in 2014 in connection to the invasion of Ukraine can be lifted on the condition that Russia implements the Minsk Peace Agreements. Moreover, in the EU all restrictive measures are kept under constant review by the Council of the EU so as to “ensure that they continue to contribute towards achieving their stated objectives”. At any time, the Council can decide to amend, extend or suspend restrictive measures. Persons or entities affected by the restrictive measures can submit requests to the Council asking for a review of the decision to be sanctioned. They also have the possibility to challenge the restrictive measures before the General Court of the European Union in accordance with the Treaty on the Functioning of the European Union (TFEU). Read similar cases of disinformation in the database claiming that Western sanctions are illegal and hostile actions, that the West needs a cause to sanction Russia, and if there isn't one, they will invent it, that the EU is obsessed with anti-Russian sanctions, that an aggressive Russophobic lobby within the EU is responsible for EU sanctions against Russia, and that Biden copied sanctions on Russia from his predecessors, based on ridiculous claims of interference.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Belarus suspended its participation in the EU Eastern Partnership (EaP) programme. This situation is a fatal blow to the main geopolitical project of the West on the post-Soviet area, which institutionally shaped the construction of the “buffer zone” between Russia and the EU. The Belarusian refusal to participate in this project disabled the EU from completing the “cordon sanitaire” between the Baltic to the Black Seas. De facto, Belarus has withdrawn from the main Western anti-Russian geopolitical project in the post-Soviet area. It is not by chance that Poland became one of the initiators of the EaP – this programme reflects the Polish geopolitical concept of the establishment of a “sanitary cordon” along Russian borders. Poland does not have sufficient resources to take back the former lands of the First Rzeczpospolita, so the EaP is the protectorate of Brussels. The Eastern Partnership programme is clearly about the “buffer zone” and “sanitary cordon”. The Belarusian decision to suspend cooperation with the Eastern Partnership is the greatest geopolitical defeat of the West since the reunification of Crimea with Russia.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the EU Eastern Partnership, which is portrayed as an “anti-Russian” and “postcolonial” tool used by the EU for alleged interference in internal affairs of the post-Soviet countries as well as the West's strategy of encircling Russia. On 26 June 2021, Belarus suspended its participation in the Eastern Partnership. This step was the Belarusian regime's reaction to the introduction of the second round of EU sanctions against Lukashenka’s regime. The Eastern Partnership (EaP) is a joint policy initiative which aims to deepen and strengthen relations between the European Union (EU), its Member States and its six Eastern neighbours: Armenia, Azerbaijan, Belarus, Georgia, Moldova, and Ukraine. The Eastern Partnership initiative is a mutually beneficial and flexible platform for countries in the region to build a closer relationship with the EU if they choose to do so. The EU does not demand any of its partners to make a choice between the EU or any other country or grouping. The Eastern Partnership stands for good neighbourly relations and respects the individual aspirations and ambitions of each partner country and is not an attempt to encircle Russia. Read more about the Eastern Partnership and its priorities here. Read similar cases concerning the Eastern Partnership here.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: While preserving peace was one of NATO’s founding principles, the Alliance has completely abandoned this practice. This is a bloc that was created for confrontation and today focuses on nothing else but confrontation. It does not bring any stability to the European continent. On the contrary, it is a force of destabilisation. Russia strives to have normal relations with NATO members, but the same cannot be said about them. The Baltic Republics are a case in point – these countries do everything they can to invite foreign military forces next to Russia’s borders. Russia is prepared to do everything in its power to stabilise relations between it and the US.
label: russia_narrative

text: A recurrent disinformation narrative that vilifies NATO as a supposedly aggressive, anti-Russian and destabilising organisation, while falsely presenting Russia as a peaceful and cooperative actor. As is typical, the pro-Kremlin outlet does not present any tangible evidence of NATO’s aggression. Contrary to the claim made in the article, NATO’s primary aims are to maintain peace and safeguard the independence, security and territorial integrity of its members, which it continues to do in the present. NATO’s presence in the Baltic countries and in other NATO members has an entirely defensive nature and is not specifically directed against Russia or any other country for that matter.Contrary to the claim, NATO’s presence in Europe brings stability to an increasingly unstable security environment, primarily due to Russia’s increasingly aggressive and threatening foreign policy. Specifically, Kremlin has engaged in numerous malign operations on the territories of NATO members in Europe, including the 2014 Vrbětice ammunitions depot explosion in Czechia, the attempted poisoning of Sergei Skripal in 2018 in the UK, and continuous interference in the internal affairs of several countries, including the UK, France, Poland, Bulgaria and others. Russia also continues to engage in unlawful behaviour that threatens the wider international community, including unlawful trade with North Korea, carrying out cyber-attacks, the proliferation of chemical and biological weapons, and the suppression of human rights, among many others. In other words, NATO serves as an important guarantor for each member’s independence and territorial integrity, even in the face of large, nuclear-armed states such as Russia. Despite the Kremlin’s widespread breaches of international norms, NATO continues to rely on its key principles of deterrence and dialogue in its relations with Russia. All practical cooperation had to be suspended in 2014 after Russia invaded Ukraine and occupied Crimea. Nevertheless, the NATO-Russia Council (NRC) has never ceased to operate and high-level political and military channels of communications remain open. As NATO has repeatedly stressed, the Alliance is open to a constructive relationship with Russia but only on the reasonable condition that Russia “demonstrates compliance with international law and Russia's international commitments”. Read similar cases of disinformation in the database claiming that NATO troops in Baltic countries and Poland threaten the security of Russia and Belarus, that NATO does not serve the purpose of war prevention, it continues to wage wars, that NATO is trying to encircle Russia and has one goal – to neutralise Russia, and that NATO is conducting aggressive anti-Russian operations.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: NATO is convinced that Crimea is Ukraine, not Russia. This means that NATO or some NATO member states, in particular the US and the UK, may reserve the right to invade Russian territory near Crimea.Despite the undeniable fact that NATO is the initiator of destabilisation in the region, NATO representatives unjustifiably accused Russia of continuing destabilisation in the region. Their only argument was that Russia is deploying weapons in Crimea, including missile systems, which complicates freedom of navigation. Needless to say, Crimea is part of Russia, its territory, Russia has the right to deploy not only missile systems but also nuclear weapons if it deems it necessary. The accusation of the NATO representative could be considered ridiculous, his words are evidence of NATO's aggressive and dangerous aspirations in a broader context.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring disinformation narrative about NATO and its military exercises, part of consistent disinformation attacks on the alliance and its members, intensified after the NATO summit. NATO is a defensive alliance. The primary aim of the alliance is to maintain peace and safeguard the independence, security and territorial integrity of its members. Тhis statement refers to NATO's multinational exercises Sea Breeze being held in the Black Sea. In reality, the Sea Breeze exercises are not aimed against Russia and pose no threat to anyone in the Black Sea region.NATO follows strict procedures and emphasises transparency during military training exercises, which are announced months in advance and are subject to international scrutiny. All NATO members and partners participate voluntarily and training exercises are conducted with the approval of the host countries. The Brussel Summit Communique, presented after the meeting of NATO heads in Belgium on 14 June 2021, reaffirmed, that "NATO does not seek confrontation and poses no threat to Russia. Decisions we have taken are fully consistent with our international commitments, and therefore cannot be regarded by anyone as contradicting the NATO-Russia Founding Act." As for the claim that Crimea is part of Russia - no international body recognises the so-called referendum, announced on 27 February 2014, and held on 16 March 2014 under the military occupation of Russian forces. For further debunk read here. Read more about the myth "Crimea was always was Russian"
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The West needs Ukrainian land to build NATO bases. Ukraine keeps on dreaming about NATO membership and is preparing for a mythical Russian invasion.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin media, claiming that NATO is a hostile bloc, one of whose main goals is to surround Russia with military bases in former Soviet republics including Ukraine.In reality, NATO is primarily a defensive and political organisation that first and foremost strives to prevent conflict rather than using military force to solve it. That is why the NATO-Ukraine partnership is based on defensive grounds. Joint military exercises help enhance the defence capabilities of the Ukrainian Armed Forces and are not aimed against any country. It is obvious that Kyiv's future membership in NATO will bring NATO bases to Ukrainian soil because it is quite natural for a member of a military alliance to host bases of this alliance. Together with that, NATO will bring stability to Ukraine in the face of Russian aggression, which is not mythical but real. It has been going on since 2014 when Russia illegally annexed the Ukrainian peninsula of Crimea and instigated a revolt in Donbas. A communiqué issued after the meeting of NATO Heads of State and Government in Belgium on 14 June 2021 said: Read more disinformation narratives about NATO’s alleged provocations against Russia and the Alliance’s presence in Eastern Ukraine. “NATO does not seek confrontation and poses no threat to Russia. Decisions we have taken are fully consistent with our international commitments, and therefore cannot be regarded by anyone as contradicting the NATO-Russia Founding Act.”
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The (Crimea) incident helped the Kremlin to confirm the narrative that Russia is surrounded by enemies.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin narrative that Russia is being encircled at the same time portraying NATO’s military exercises as security threats. The claim that NATO is encircling Russia is one of the myths pushed about NATO. Russia's land border is just over 20,000 kilometres long. Of that, less than one-sixteenth (1,215 kilometres), is shared with NATO members. Russia has land borders with 14 countries. Only five of them are NATO members. Outside NATO territory, the Alliance only has a military presence in two places: Kosovo and Afghanistan. Sea Breeze is a multinational military exercise that has been held in Ukraine since 1997 in order to enhance its defensive capability and to bring the Ukrainian fleet closer to NATO standards. This year’s Sea Breeze exercises are taking place from June 28 to July 10 2021 in the Black Sea region and have led to a flood of accusations about their intent from pro-Kremlin media. NATO poses no threat to Russia, and this was reaffirmed by all allies in the Brussels Summit Communiqué of 2021:"NATO does not seek confrontation and poses no threat to Russia. Decisions we have taken are fully consistent with our international commitments, and therefore cannot be regarded by anyone as contradicting the NATO-Russia Founding Act". Furthermore, the so-called "incident" referred to in the article didn't really occur. On 23 June 2021, the UK Royal Navy was conducting innocent passage through Ukrainian territorial waters in accordance with international law." The ship, HMS Defender, carried out a routine transit from Odesa towards Georgia across the Black Sea through internationally recognised Ukrainian territorial waters. Read similar cases in our database that claims that the UK was ready to create a casus belli in the Black Sea; or that UK Destroyer's passage near Crimea violated international law; or that UK warship presence in the Black Sea was a NATO provocation; or even that the UK wanted Russia to shoot at the HMS Defender.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The UK violated the 1982 law of the Seas Treaty when its destroyer "Defender" entered Russian territorial waters in a provocative manner. Crimea has returned to the Russian body with a popular referendum, and Russian President Vladimir Putin has confirmed that the Crimea issue has been closed, which is a fair decision.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the status of Crimea and the maritime movement in the Black Sea. The British Ministry of Defense denied that the “provocation incident” had occurred or that the ship was in Russian waters, stating that the HMS Defender was engaged in innocent passage through the Ukrainian territorial sea in accordance with international law, as reflected in the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS). UK Foreign Secretary Dominic Raab told reporters, “… We were doing so in accordance with international law and the Russian characterisation is predictably inaccurate.” All ships, including warships, enjoy a right of innocent passage through the territorial sea under UNCLOS, Article 17. The passage is innocent so long as it is not prejudicial to the peace, good order or security of the coastal state. Until this date, there is no evidence that the British destroyer was engaged in any prohibited activity at the time of the alleged incident. Rather, British authorities indicated that the HMS Defender was conducting a routine transit from Odesa to Georgia using an internationally recognised traffic separation corridor in the Black Sea. Russia has occupied Crimea in March 2014, and so far no international body has recognised the so-called popular referendum in Crimea thus the illegal annexation of the area, not the UN, nor the EU or the UK. See similar disinformation cases about the occupation of Crimea in our database such as the Crimea issue is finally closed following the results of the referendum, Crimea - peacefully and legitimately - became part of Russia; or that Russia occupied Crimea to keep NATO ships out; or that the alleged coup d'etat of Maidan caused a referendum that united Crimea with Russia.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: ... Crimea became part of Russia. In 1781 the Crimean Khanate joined the Russian Empire, and since then, the majority of the peninsula's population have become Russians, ethnic Russians, moreover, in 2014, the vote for accession to Russia was voted on by most residents of this area. That is why when Western ships, including the British destroyer, came and passed through the territorial waters of Crimea and said that they are in Ukrainian territorial waters, this is nonsense and a falsification of reality, which is dangerous and even more so than the Cold War era.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the illegal annexation of Crimea, claiming that it voted to rejoin Russia through a legal referendum and painting the idea that it never belonged to Ukraine, and the UK destroyer entered Russian territorial waters. For historical reference, Crimea was annexed by Catherine II (the Great), then containing Ukraine in 1783, already in violation of a treaty that guaranteed the independence of the Tatar khanate. In 1954, in the USSR, Crimea was officially made a part of the Ukraine Soviet Republic.After the collapse of the USSR, Russia reaffirmed respect for the territorial integrity and sovereignty of Ukraine, by signing the 1997 Friendship Treaty, Russia also recognised that Crimea belongs to Ukraine. Regarding the so-called referendum announced on the 27th of February 2014, and held on the 16th of March 2014, it was organised hastily and at gunpoint following the covert invasion of “little green men” and barring impartial observers from entering the peninsula can hardly be called "reunification in strict accordance with all the norms of international law. Ukrainian authorities reported almost in real-time the identification and details of the Russian military forces invading Crimea. Even Vladimir Putin, the President of Russia, recognised on 17 April 2015 that "our soldiers were deployed in Crimea to help the inhabitants express their opinion." Putin admitted (and was proud of) that the plan to annex Crimea was ordered weeks before the so-called referendum. No international body recognises the so-called referendum in Crimea. The UN General Assembly adopted a resolution entitled “Territorial integrity of Ukraine”, stating that the referendum in Crimea was not valid and could not serve as a basis for any change in the status of the peninsula. On 17 December 2018, the UN General Assembly confirmed its non-recognition of the illegal annexation of Crimea. The European Union does not recognise and continues to strongly condemn this violation of international law, which remains a challenge to the international security order. Furthermore, and until this date, there is no evidence that the British destroyer has engaged in any prohibited activity at the time of the alleged incident. Rather, British authorities indicated that HMS Defender was conducting a routine transit from Odesa to Georgia using an internationally recognised traffic separation corridor in the Black Sea inside Ukrainian territorial waters. Check previous disinformation narratives in our database alleging that the EU has accepted Crimea's incorporation into Russia and that the Crimean people chose to be with Russia through the democratic procedure; or that historically Crimea was a Russian land; or that Moscow did not violate international law when reunifying Crimea with Russia, or that the UK was ready to create a casus belli in the Black Sea; or that UK Destroyer's passage near Crimea violated international law; or that UK warship presence in the Black Sea was a NATO provocation;
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The relations between Moscow and Brussels is the hostage of the Russophobic approach of the “Young Europeans” and Poland, which consistently escalate tensions in relations with Russia. These sides take quite an irreconcilable position. These countries speak the most about their concerns about the ephemeral threat they see in the Russian Federation. At the same time, the same “Young Europeans” [new EU member states] consistently follow the path of escalating tensions in relations with Russia.
label: russia_narrative

text: This message is a part of the Kremlin’s widespread narrative about anti-Russian, Russophobic countries of Eastern and Central Europe, deliberately worsening their relations with Russia, preventing the improvement of the EU-Russian relations and supporting American interests in the region. The claim that the new EU member states somehow escalate tensions with Russia and prevent the improvement of the EU-Russian relations is ungrounded. During the recent months, the Russian officials and pro-Kremlin media increasingly use the euphemism “Young Europeans” (“младоевропейцы”) to describe the new EU member states (countries of Eastern and Central Europe). In the Russian language, this euphemism directly reminds of the “Young Turks” movement (“младотурки”). The dramatic worsening of relations between the EU and Russia took place in 2014 after the illegal annexation of Crimea and direct Russian interference in the conflict in Donbas. The recent deterioration of relations between Russia and the EU is directly linked to Moscow’s illegal actions abroad (violation of international law) and oppression against representatives of civil society inside Russia. Since March 2014, the EU has progressively imposed restrictive measures against Russia in response to the illegal annexation of Crimea and the deliberate destabilisation of Ukraine. New sanctions were introduced after the assassination attempt on Russian opposition activist Alexei Navalny in 2020. The EU has also sanctioned Russian individuals responsible for serious human rights violations, including arbitrary arrests and detentions, as well as widespread and systematic repression of freedom of peaceful assembly and of association, and freedom of opinion and expression in Russia. See similar cases such as Russia-EU relations were destroyed by unilateral decisions of Brussels, The EU destroyed relations with Russia and Poland and the EU appear on the verge of breaking relations with Russia.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: A day after Russian border control fired warning shots at a British warship, a new [Dutch] provocator appeared near the Crimean peninsula.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring disinformation narrative spread by pro-Kremlin outlets aimed at justifying the Kremlin's aggression against Ukraine's territorial integrity and at portraying European countries as provocative parties to the Ukrainian crisis. On 29 June 2021, the Defence Ministry of the Netherlands reported that Russian warplanes repeatedly flew low over a Dutch navy frigate in the Black Sea and carried out “mock attacks”. The incident involved the Dutch ship Evertsen southeast of Crimea that has been occupied by Russian forces since February 2014.The incident came a day after Russian forces fired warning shots at a British destroyer called HMS Defender to force it out of an area near Crimea that Russia claims as its territorial waters. Reiterating the Russian navy's explanation, the British government said that the destroyer "was conducting innocent passage through Ukrainian territorial waters in accordance with international law". The Dutch Evertsen was patrolling in the Black Sea with HMS Defender, the British ship that was involved in that incident. While Russia claims Crimea to be its own territory, the vast majority of the international community refuses to recognise the Kremlin's authority. Russia occupied Crimea in March 2014. No international body has recognised the so-called referendum in Crimea in 2014, not the UN, nor the EU or the UK. The UNGA Resolution 68/262, which condemned Russia's illegal actions in Ukraine, was supported by 100 United Nations member states. Only 10 states opposed it. See similar cases of disinformation spread by pro-Kremlin media outlets about the case of the HMS Defender here.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Lithuania, Poland, and other eastern European countries view the Eastern Partnership as an institutional cordon sanitaire and a buffer zone between Russia and Europe. They needed Belarus to take part in the Eastern Partnership, because without Belarus the chain of anti-Russian countries between the Baltic and Black Seas breaks down. Belarus's decision to suspend its membership in the Eastern Partnership is the greatest western geopolitical loss in eastern Europe since Crimea joined Russia.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the EU Eastern Partnership, which is portrayed as an “anti-Russian” tool used by the EU for alleged interference in internal affairs of the post-Soviet countries as well as the West's strategy of encircling Russia. On 26 June 2021, Belarus suspended its participation in the Eastern Partnership. This step was the Belarusian regime's reaction to the introduction of EU sanctions against Lukashenka’s regime. The Eastern Partnership (EaP) is a joint policy initiative which aims to deepen and strengthen relations between the European Union (EU), its Member States and its six Eastern neighbours: Armenia, Azerbaijan, Belarus, Georgia, Moldova, and Ukraine. The Eastern Partnership initiative is a mutually beneficial and flexible platform for countries in the region to build a closer relationship with the EU if they choose to do so. The EU does not demand any of its partners to make a choice between the EU or any other country or grouping. The Eastern Partnership stands for good neighbourly relations and respects the individual aspirations and ambitions of each partner country and is not an attempt to encircle Russia. Read more about the Eastern Partnership and its priorities here. The article also claims that Crimea joined Russia legitimately, a claim debunked in detail on this website. Read earlier disinformation cases alleging that the EaP is a tool against the Commonwealth of Independent States, that it is about external control and disintegration, and that the Eastern Partnership aims to erode Belarusian identity and promote the views of Polish gentry.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The conflict in eastern Ukraine began in 2014 when Kyiv launched an attack against the Donetsk and Luhansk regions after they unilaterally declared their independence.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring disinformation message about eastern Ukraine, claiming that the aggression came from Kyiv and that local dwellers resisted alone without Russian military support and founded the DPR and LPR. Ukraine is not at war with millions of people from Donetsk and Luhansk as pro-Kremlin disinformation often likes to present it. Donetsk and Luhansk are part of Ukraine and are controlled by Russia-backed armed formations. Not a single country has recognised them as independent. These messages aim to present the situation in Eastern Ukraine as an internal conflict, ignoring irrefutable evidence of direct Russian military involvement in the region. These claims intentionally misrepresent the reality of Russia's direct and crucial support to separatist militant groups in Eastern Ukraine. The involvement of Russian personnel and equipment in the creation of the DPR and LPR has been well documented. The destabilisation of Eastern Ukraine and the illegal annexation of Crimea has been condemned by the Council of the European Union and led to restrictive measures since 2014. The EU has stressed on several occasions that Ukraine’s borders must be respected, and the same goes for the UN. Read similar stories in our database claiming that a civil war began in Ukraine after the clique seized power in the country, or that the Russian Army is not involved in the war in Ukraine; or that Crimea never belonged to Ukraine; or that Ukraine is only responsible for civil war in Donbas; or that the alleged coup in Kyiv pushed DNR and LNR to be independent.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: NATO has turned Ukraine into a huge anti-Russian launchpad, which makes Russia revise its approach towards security issues.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin media, trying to portray NATO as a hostile organisation and Ukraine as a tool for Western aggression against Russia. In reality, Ukraine’s cooperation with NATO is not levelled against Russia. Kyiv’s partnership with NATO and its participation in Sea Breeze military exercises in particular are aimed at enhancing Ukraine’s defence capabilities. Kyiv is striving for good relations with all of its neighbours including Moscow, but Russia must first withdraw from the illegally annexed peninsula Crimea and the separatist-held areas of Donbas. Ukraine aims to secure the liberation of these territories with peaceful means, such as the Minsk agreements and Normandy format talks. While Ukraine and its Western partners have been striving to make the Black Sea region safer and more stable since the outbreak of Russian aggression in 2014, Russia has been continually engaging in provocative behaviour (see here and here). It has been militarising the illegally annexed peninsula of Crimea. These activities are adversely affecting the security situation in the region.This year’s Sea Breeze exercises are taking place from June 28 to July 10 in the Black Sea region. They have the largest number of participating nations in the exercises’ history with 32 countries from six continents providing 5,000 troops, 32 ships, 40 aircraft, and 18 special operations and dive teams. Ukraine has been cooperating with NATO since the early 1990s. This partnership has been intensified in critical areas since 2014, in the wake of the Russia-Ukraine conflict. In June 2017, the Ukrainian Parliament adopted legislation reinstating membership in NATO as a strategic foreign and security policy objective. In 2019, a corresponding amendment to Ukraine’s Constitution entered into force. Read more disinformation narratives from pro-Kremlin media about NATO’s alleged provocative behaviour towards Russia.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The beginning of the military absorption of Ukraine by foreign countries is a serious threat to Russia’s territorial integrity.
label: russia_narrative

text: A recurring disinformation narrative that claims the West is using Ukraine as a military staging ground for an attack on Russia. This narrative serves to divert attention away from and justify Russia’s unlawful actions, including its ongoing involvement in the war in Ukraine and the annexation of Crimea in 2014. There is no tangible evidence for any sort of “military absorption” of Ukraine by the West. Western states and organisations are fully transparent about the aid they provide to Ukraine in support of its self-defence. This is in contrast to Russia, which continues to deny that it supports the separatist forces in Eastern Ukraine directly and indirectly in breach of international law. Since the start of hostilities in 2014, the US has supported Ukraine indirectly by providing Ukraine with military equipment and by training its soldiers. NATO officers stationed in Ukraine help in the reformation of the Ukrainian armed forces, which sometimes participate in joint exercises with NATO. The EU also supports Ukraine without direct military involvement by providing humanitarian and financial aid and by supporting Ukraine’s reform programme.Moreover, Ukraine is an independent country and is free to engage in bilateral or multilateral relations under international law with any country or organisation it wishes. The West’s actions are a reaction to Russia’s increasingly hostile foreign policy and have an entirely defensive nature, seeking to contain any further breaches of Ukraine’s sovereignty and territorial integrity. During the last meeting of the NATO-Ukraine Commission in April 2021, both Ukraine and NATO underlined the importance of the Minsk Agreements and urged Russia to withdraw its forces from Eastern Ukraine. Both the EU and the US have repeatedly stated that their sanctions against Russia are aimed at encouraging Russia to take a diplomatic approach and implement the Minsk Agreements. In other words, neither the West nor Ukraine pose a threat to Russia’s territorial integrity. Detailed and accurate overviews of cooperation between Ukraine and NATO, the EU and the US are available online. Read similar cases of disinformation in the database claiming that the US and their NATO allies are turning Ukraine into a "powder keg", that NATO will supply Ukraine with modern weapons, that Defender Europe 2021 is training for an offensive against Russia and that the goal of Ukraine-NATO military exercise is to kill Russians.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The HMS Defender’s violation of Russian territorial waters was a planned provocation by the UK and the US.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurrent pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives about the HMS Defender incident. The article’s message is also consistent with pro-Kremlin narratives about the status of Crimea and about the US provoking Russia. It is not true that the UK and US provoked Russia with the transit of the British ship HMS Defender through the Black Sea towards Georgia. There is no evidence to support the claim that this was a planned provocation. The Black Sea transit of the HMS Defender on the Odesa to Georgia route is within full legal maritime law guaranteed as the “right of innocent passage” by the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), to which both the UK and Russia are parties. The British Minister of Defence officially stated that: According to the UNCLOS articles, the HMS Defender's crossing, as well as being in line with one of the most commonly used routes in the Black Sea, is legitimately within the terms of innocent passage through the waters off the Crimean peninsula and there has been no report, record or clear evidence of any provocative or aggressive approach from the UK ship to occupied Crimea. The allegation that the HMS violated Russian territorial waters is a manipulation of the issue of Crimea. The HMS Defender transited 12 miles offshore in the waters of Crimea, a territory militarily occupied and forcibly annexed by Russia in 2014 in a referendum that was not recognised by the UN, nor the EU or the UK or any international body. Innocent passage of the HMS Defender through the waters off Cape Fiolent on the Southern tip of Crimea is allowed under international law: Russia's accusation is based on the assumption that its self-asserted right over Crimean territory is recognised, but neither Ukraine nor the international community currently recognises Crimea as Russian territory. Russia's illegitimate control over the territories of the Crimean peninsula has manifestly extended to the Kerch Strait and the Sea of Azov, where military incidents have occurred. Read more about the Kerch Strait issue here and here, while a more in-depth look at Russia's expansion into the Sea of Azov can be read here. Read similar cases claiming that with the support of the US, the UK was ready to create a casus belli in the Black Sea, that UK ship HMS Defender violated Russian territorial waters in Crimea and that following the results of the referendum, Crimea - peacefully and legitimately - became part of Russia The Royal Navy ship is conducting innocent passage through Ukrainian territorial waters in accordance with international law.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The US is evaluating Ukraine as a dangerous and non-valuable ally. Washington also should take responsibility for the coup in Ukraine in 2014. The US helped ultranationalists' and Neo-Nazis' groups to overthrow the government. The situation with human rights in Ukraine also is horrible. Kyiv is trying to silence critics. Ukraine is a trap for the US. Kyiv could push Washington to needless war.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring disinformation about Ukraine, presenting this state as the puppet of the US. The official relations between the US and Ukraine were established in 1991 after the collapse of the Soviet Union. At the moment, the US policy is centred on supporting Ukraine in the face of continued Russian aggression as it advances reforms to strengthen democratic institutions, fight corruption, and promote conditions for economic growth and competition. There was no far-right coup d’état in Ukraine in 2014; this is a longstanding pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about Ukraine's Euromaidan protests. The spontaneous onset of the Euromaidan protests was a reaction from several segments of the Ukrainian population to former President Yanukovych’s sudden departure from the promised Association Agreement with the European Union in November 2013, after pressure from Russia. Ukraine has some issues in the sphere of human rights but the situation is far away from “horrible”. In the “Freedom of the World 2021” list, Ukraine is marked as a “partly free” country. See more information about the real situation with human rights in this country here. See similar examples of disinformation: The coup d’état of Maidan caused a referendum that united Crimea with Russia; The far-right coup government in Kyiv emerged from the 2014 Maidan uprising; Maidan-coup part of propagandistic marching baggage for the US-lead World order.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Less than a week before its launch (Exercise Sea Breeze 2021), a resounding incident occurred with the British destroyer Defender, which flagrantly violated Russia's maritime border near Sevastopol.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the status of Crimea and the passage of HMS Defender through waters near Crimea. On 23 June 2021, the UK Ministry of Defence Press Office stated that "the Royal Navy ship [was] conducting innocent passage through Ukrainian territorial waters in accordance with international law." It also added that the ship "carried out a routine transit from Odesa towards Georgia across the Black Sea" through internationally recognised Ukrainian territorial waters. The UK Ministry of Defence also denied the claims that the HMS Defender has been fired on. The crew of HMS Defender maneuvered the vessel in strict observance of international protocol. By passing near Crimea, the Defender was exercising the right of innocent passage, as laid down in the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea. Articles 17-19 of the Convention allow for passage through another state's territorial sea, provided it is "continuous and expeditious" and "not prejudicial to the peace, good order or security of the coastal State" (pp. 30-1). Innocent passage is one of several qualifications on the absolute sovereignty of a coastal state over its territorial waters. This means that a vessel exercising this right within the meaning of the Convention does not need to seek permission of the coastal state. This, according to a leading maritime law scholar, "makes it irrelevant to which state the territory in question belongs". See this article by POLYGRAPH.info for additional debunking and earlier examples of Moscow's misleading coverage of maritime incidents in the Black Sea. Russia has occupied Crimea in March 2014, and so far no international body has recognised the so-called referendum in Crimea thus illegal annexation of the area, not the UN, nor the EU or the UK. The UK's Ambassador Neil Bush mentioned at the OSCE Permanent Council meeting in March 2021 that Crimea was blatantly occupied by the Russian military "in violation of its OSCE principles and commitments." See similar disinformation cases about this incident such as UK Destroyer entered Russian waters and only left after warning shots and about the occupation of Crimea such as the Crimea issue is finally closed following the results of the referendum, Crimea - peacefully and legitimately - became part of Russia; or that Russia occupied Crimea to keep NATO ships out; or that the alleged coup d'etat of Maidan caused a referendum that united Crimea with Russia.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: NATO is working out a possible military operation along Russia’s border during the Sea Breeze military exercises. Psychological and information warfare against Russia is part of NATO’s strategy. If a political crisis breaks out in Russia, an intervention may be carried out efficiently. These military exercises threaten not only Crimea but Russia as a whole.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin media, claiming that NATO is an aggressive military bloc, whose goal is to destroy Russia or at least to harm it as much as possible. In reality, NATO is a political alliance, which promotes democratic values, builds trust and, in the long run, prevents conflict. Its military exercises, including this year's Sea Breeze, have an entirely defensive nature and are not directed against Russia or any other country. Ukraine's cooperation with NATO is also defensive, whose aim is to enhance Kyiv’s defence capabilities. The exercises were announced in advance and are taking place from 28 June to 10 July 2021 in the Black Sea region. They have the largest number of participating nations in the exercises’ history with 32 countries from six continents providing 5,000 troops, 32 ships, 40 aircraft, and 18 special operations and dive teams scheduled to participate. The increase in the number of NATO’s exercises over the last years is a defensive reaction to the unstable security environment in Europe, especially due to Russia’s increasingly aggressive foreign policy. However, the Alliance is intent on settling all problems with Moscow peacefully. The communiqué, issued after the NATO summit in Brussels on 14 June 2021, reiterated the alliance’s support for the territorial integrity and sovereignty of Ukraine, Georgia, and the Republic of Moldova within their internationally recognised borders. The document also said: At the same time, NATO did not rule out dialogue with Russia: See more disinformation narratives about NATO’s alleged presence in Donbas and its provocative actions against Russia. “We strongly condemn and will not recognise Russia’s illegal and illegitimate annexation of Crimea, and denounce its temporary occupation.” “We remain open to a periodic, focused, and meaningful dialogue with a Russia willing to engage on the basis of reciprocity in the NRC, with a view to avoiding misunderstanding, miscalculation, and unintended escalation, and to increase transparency and predictability.”
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The Russian Ministry of Defense reported that the British destroyer "Defender" entered Russian territorial waters off the coast of Crimea and left after a Russian border patrol ship fired warning shots, and a SU-24M aircraft dropped warning bombs on the destroyer's sailing line.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the status of Crimea and the passage of HMS Defender through waters near Crimea. On 23 June 2021, the UK Ministry of Defence Press Office stated that "the Royal Navy ship [was] conducting innocent passage through Ukrainian territorial waters in accordance with international law." It also added that the ship "carried out a routine transit from Odesa towards Georgia across the Black Sea" through internationally recognised Ukrainian territorial waters. The UK Ministry of Defence also denied the claims that the HMS Defender has been fired on. The crew of HMS Defender maneuvered the vessel in strict observance of international protocol. By passing near Crimea, the Defender was exercising the right of innocent passage, as laid down in the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea. Articles 17-19 of the Convention allow for passage through another state's territorial sea, provided it is "continuous and expeditious" and "not prejudicial to the peace, good order or security of the coastal State" (pp. 30-1). Innocent passage is one of several qualifications on the absolute sovereignty of a coastal state over its territorial waters. This means that a vessel exercising this right within the meaning of the Convention does not need to seek permission of the coastal state. This, according to a leading maritime law scholar, "makes it irrelevant to which state the territory in question belongs". See this article by POLYGRAPH.info for additional debunking and earlier examples of Moscow's misleading coverage of maritime incidents in the Black Sea. Russia has occupied Crimea in March 2014, and so far no international body has recognised the so-called referendum in Crimea thus illegal annexation of the area, not the UN, nor the EU or the UK. The UK's Ambassador Neil Bush mentioned at the OSCE Permanent Council meeting in March 2021 that Crimea was blatantly occupied by the Russian military "in violation of its OSCE principles and commitments." See similar disinformation cases about this incident such as UK Destroyer entered Russian waters and only left after warning shots and about the occupation of Crimeasuch as the Crimea issue is finally closed following the results of the referendum, Crimea - peacefully and legitimately - became part of Russia; or that Russia occupied Crimea to keep NATO ships out; or that the alleged coup d'etat of Maidan caused a referendum that united Crimea with Russia.

text: The Ukrainian-American military exercise Sea Breeze - 2021, which started today in the Black Sea, is nothing more than a rehearsal for the quick takeover of Crimea.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation alleging that military cooperation between Ukraine and NATO is directed against Russia.Sea Breeze is a multinational military exercise that has been held in Ukraine since 1997 in line with the 1993 Memorandum of Understanding and Cooperation in Defense and Military Relations between the U.S. Department of Defense and the Ministry of Defense of Ukraine. The exercises are co-organised by Ukraine and the United States. This year’s Sea Breeze exercises are taking place from June 28 to July 10 2021 in the Black Sea region. They have the largest number of participating nations in the exercises’ history with 32 countries from six continents providing 5,000 troops, 32 ships, 40 aircraft, and 18 special operations and dive teams. This year the focus is on "multiple warfare areas including amphibious warfare, land manoeuvre warfare, diving operations, maritime interdiction operations, air defence, special operations integration, anti-submarine warfare, and search and rescue operations." Moreover, the goal is "to gain experience in joint actions during multinational peacekeeping and security operations."NATO is not preparing a war against Russia (or any other country). NATO is a defensive alliance, whose purpose is to protect its member states. The primary aim of the alliance is to maintain peace and safeguard the independence, security, and territorial integrity of its members. NATO follows strict procedures and emphasises transparency during military training exercises, which are announced months in advance and are subject to international scrutiny. All NATO members and partners participate voluntarily and training exercises are conducted with the approval of the host countries. The Brussel Summit Communique, presented after the meeting of NATO heads in Belgium on 14 June 2021, reaffirmed, that "NATO does not seek confrontation and poses no threat to Russia. Decisions we have taken are fully consistent with our international commitments, and therefore cannot be regarded by anyone as contradicting the NATO-Russia Founding Act." See similar disinformation cases in our database: Sea Breeze exercise is a provocative NATO attack on Russia in the Black Sea region; NATO uses Sea Breeze exercise to secretly send arms to Donbas; NATO to deliver arms to Kyiv under the guise of Sea Breeze war games; Sea Breeze exercises are directed against Russia.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: In March 2018, Poroshenko sent his military vessels towards the Crimea bridge. One of the goals of that provocation, was to cancel Putin's meting with Trump suring the G20 meeting in Argentina. The provocation was successful [...]Now Boris Johnson is acting like Petro Poroshenko. This is "Ukrainisation" of British politics, with the close support of the US. Both Americans and the Brits knew exactly what they were doing. They knew they were crossing a well-guarded Russian border and knew the risks.They were ready to create casus belli -a pretext for war.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the incident with the HMS Defender in the Black Sea, accusing the UK of provoking Russia. There is no evidence to back these claims.On 23 June, the UK Ministry of Defence Press Office stated that "the Royal Navy ship [was] conducting innocent passage through Ukrainian territorial waters in accordance with international law." It also added that the ship "carried out a routine transit from Odesa towards Georgia across the Black Sea" through internationally recognised Ukrainian territorial waters.The UK Ministry of Defence also denied the claims that the HMS Defender has been fired on by the Russian side. The crew of HMS Defender maneuvered the vessel in strict observance of international protocol. By passing near Crimea, the Defender was exercising the right of innocent passage, as laid down in the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea. Articles 17-19 of the Convention allow for passage through another state's territorial sea, provided it is "continuous and expeditious" and "not prejudicial to the peace, good order or security of the coastal State" (pp. 30-1). 'Innocent passage' is one of several qualifications on the absolute sovereignty of a coastal state over its territorial waters. This means that a vessel exercising this right within the meaning of the Convention does not need to seek permission of the coastal state. This, according to a leading maritime law scholar, "makes it irrelevant to which state the territory in question belongs". Russia occupied Crimea in March 2014. No international body has recognised the so-called referendum in Crimea, not the UN, nor the EU or the UK.Read more about the incident here.Read more about pro-Kremlin disinformation surrounding the Kerch strait incident here.See related examples of disinformation here.
