text: NATO provokes Russia in front of its border. Contrary to the promises made to Gorbachev, NATO has advanced to the Russian border, and as thanks for the dissolution of the Warsaw Pact and the reunification of Germany, Russia continues to be encircled and provoked with maneuvers (Defender 21) in front of its border.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative, claiming that NATO is waging an aggressive campaign against Russia.NATO exercises are not aimed against Russia. Their aim is to enhance mutual defence capabilities and make the situation in the region more stable. Since NATO is a military organisation, it is obvious that it stages war games, such as Defender 2021, from time to time. However, it is also a political alliance that promotes democratic values and enables its members to consult and cooperate on defence and security-related issues to solve problems, build trust and, in the long run, prevent conflict. It is Russia that is continually militarising the region. Since 2014, it has been widely criticised for staging provocative military exercises along Ukraine’s border and in the vicinity of the Baltic nations, which are NATO members. In response to this criticism, Russia says it has the right to stage war games in its territory. At the same time, Moscow is blasting NATO for staging joint military exercises, which in fact pose no threat to anyone in the region. Concerning the promises made to Gorbachev, the statement has been debunked numerous times. NATO did not promise not to expand into eastern and central Europe back in 1990, which was confirmed by the former president of the Soviet Union Mikhail Gorbachev. Back in 2014, Gorbachev said: Moreover, the claim about NATO "expansion" misrepresents the process of NATO enlargement. NATO does not "expand" but considers the applications of candidate countries that want to join. See more narratives about NATO’s alleged belligerent agenda against Russia.This disinformation message appeared in the same article as the claim that Crimea was annexed as the result of the US-financed coup in Ukraine. "The topic of ‘NATO expansion’ was not discussed at all, and it wasn’t brought up in those years. I say this with full responsibility."
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The first task of NATO’s Sea Breeze military exercises will be destabilisation on the perimeter of the Russian border. The second one will be to bring different weapons and military equipment to the territory of Ukraine and leave them there.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin media outlets, claiming that NATO’s Sea Breeze military exercises pose a threat to Russia and that the West is using Ukraine to achieve this end.In reality, the Sea Breeze maritime drills are of a purely defensive nature. Ukraine seeks to increase its defence capabilities by taking part in them. What has been really threatening stability in the region is Russia’s recent military build-up along Ukraine’s eastern and southern border. Since its illegal annexation of Crimea in 2014, Russia has been continually militarising the Ukrainian peninsula. This year’s Sea Breeze exercises are taking place from June 28 to July 10 2021 in the Black Sea region. They have the largest number of participating nations in the exercises’ history with 32 countries from six continents providing 5,000 troops, 32 ships, 40 aircraft, and 18 special operations and dive teams. NATO is not smuggling military equipment to Ukraine under the guise of these war games. The fact that NATO commanders visit Kyiv does not mean that weapons or other military equipment are being smuggled into Ukraine or that the West coerces Ukraine into confrontation with Russia. Ukraine receives Western military aid legally and openly. Earlier this year, the United States announced a military aid package to Ukraine worth $125 million. Unlike Ukraine’s openness in its military transactions with foreign countries, Russia clandestinely provides its proxies in Donbas with military aid. Although Russia denies it, there is plenty of documented evidence to this effect. Relations between NATO and Ukraine date back to the early 1990s and have since developed into one of the most substantial of NATO’s partnerships. Since 2014, in the wake of the Russia-Ukraine conflict, cooperation has been intensified in critical areas. The alliance firmly believes that a sovereign, independent and stable Ukraine, firmly committed to democracy and the rule of law, is key to Euro-Atlantic security. The Brussel Summit Communique, presented after the meeting of NATO heads in Belgium on 14 June 2021, reaffirmed, that "NATO does not seek confrontation and poses no threat to Russia. Decisions we have taken are fully consistent with our international commitments, and therefore cannot be regarded by anyone as contradicting the NATO-Russia Founding Act." See more narratives about NATO’s allegedly provocative behaviour in relations with Russia.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Crimea was annexed as the result of the US-financed coup in Ukraine. Crimea would not have been annexed if the US had not financed the coup in Ukraine with five billion.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the illegal annexation of Crimea and Ukraine's Euromaidan protests, depicting the 2013-14 revolution as a coup d'état and laying responsibility for the ensuing Russian aggression on the West. There was no coup d’état in Kyiv in 2014. The spontaneous onset of the Euromaidan protests was not provoked externally, rather, it was a reaction from several segments of the Ukrainian population to former President Yanukovych’s sudden departure from the promised Association Agreement with the European Union in November 2013, after pressure from Russia. No international body recognises the so-called referendum in Crimea, neither the UN nor the EU. On the seventh anniversary of Crimea's annexation, the EU reiterated its position of non-recognition of illegal annexation and continues to stand in full solidarity with Ukraine, supporting its sovereignty and territorial integrity. A year after the illegal annexation of Crimea, Russian President Vladimir Putin admitted that the plan to annex Crimea was ordered weeks before the so-called referendum.Read similar disinformation narratives alleging that the US organised a coup in Ukraine and European countries submissively supported it and that EU and US interference and coup in Ukraine caused the armed conflict in Donbas.This disinformation message appeared in the same article as the claim that NATO provokes Russia in front of its border.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The West had organised the coup against president Yanukovych and thus "provoked the split within Ukraine and the departure of Crimea from the Ukrainian state."
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurrent narrative on the role of the US and the EU in 2014 events in Ukraine. In 2014, there was no coup, nor a western orchestrated protest in Ukraine. The demonstrations which began in Kyiv in November 2013 – called "Maidan", or "Euromaidan" – were a result of the Ukrainian people's frustration with former President Yanukovych's last-minute U-turn when, after seven years of negotiation, he refused to sign the EU–Ukraine Association Agreement and halted progress towards Ukraine's closer relationship with the EU due to Russian pressure. It cannot be labelled as a coup, even though pro-Kremlin media are very persistent in doing so. The protesters' demands included constitutional reform, a stronger role for parliament, the formation of a government of national unity, an end to corruption, early presidential elections and an end to violence. Ukraine's government changed its relations toward Russia after the latter annexed Crimea, which is part of Ukraine, illegally following the covert invasion of “little green men". The EU is among the strongest advocates of Ukraine's sovereignty, independence, and territorial integrity. The EU and the US work closely with Ukraine and support the democratic development of Ukraine in accordance with the UN Charter’s principles of non-interference into internal affairs and respect towards territorial integrity and political independence. See similar disinformation cases claiming that Euromaidan protests were organised by foreign governments: Gauleiter Biden and the US were behind the Orange revolution 2004 and the coup of 2014; Obama staged a coup in Ukraine, allowing Nazis to assume power; the US organised a coup in 2014 in Kyiv and now wants Ukraine to start a war against Russia.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: A bloody coup in Ukraine (happened) in 2014, with the support of the United States of America, NATO and the European Union.
label: russia_narrative

text: A recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative depicting the 2013-14 protests in Kyiv as a coup d’état. There was no coup d'état in Ukraine, nor interference from the US, EU and NATO. The spontaneous onset of the Euromaidan protests was a reaction by broad segments of the Ukrainian society to former president Viktor Yanukovych’s sudden departure from the promised Association Agreement with the European Union in November 2013. On the other hand, the war in eastern Ukraine is a well-documented act of aggression by Russian armed forces ongoing since February 2014. The same can be applied to what happened in February–March 2014 which can be described as a full-spectrum military operation executed on land and at sea while holding a referendum at gunpoint to legitimise Russia’s takeover of Crimea. This case contained another claim that Western schools are teaching students that Jesus was bisexual. Read similar stories in our database claiming that a civil war began in Ukraine after the clique seized power in the country; or the Crimean people chose to be with Russia through the democratic procedure; or that Crimea’s reunification with Russia was legal and that Crimea is a Russian sovereign region; or that the West organised the Maidan coup to make Ukraine an anti-Russian state.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: In 2014 Crimea voted for its reattachment to Russia. Still, a good number of Western capitals do not recognise this referendum as legitimate, and London is one of them.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurrent pro-Kremlin deceptive narrative on Crimea’s illegal annexation. Only a few countries worldwide recognise Russian sovereignty over Crimea as Sputnik listed: Afghanistan, Bolivia, North Korea, Cuba, Kyrgyzstan, Nicaragua, Syria, Sudan and Zimbabwe.No international body recognises the so-called referendum in Crimea. The UN General Assembly adopted a resolution entitled “Territorial integrity of Ukraine”, stating that the referendum in Crimea was not valid and could not serve as a basis for any change in the status of the peninsula. On 17 December 2018, the UN General Assembly confirmed its non-recognition of the illegal annexation of Crimea. Vladimir Putin, the President of Russia, recognised on 17 April 2015 that "our soldiers were deployed in Crimea to help the inhabitants express their opinion." Vladimir Putin admitted (proudly) that the plan to annex Crimea was ordered weeks before even the Maidan protests culminated. The European Union does not recognise and continues to strongly condemn this violation of international law, which remains a challenge to the international security order. In response to the illegal annexation of Crimea, the EU has imposed restrictive measures against the Russian Federation. In June 2021, the European Council decided to renew the sanctions introduced in response to the illegal annexation of Crimea and Sevastopol by the Russian Federation until 23 June 2022. Read also similar stories: The Crimea issue is finally closed, Crimea returned to Russia after a referendum, Crimea, Donbas separated from Ukraine after Nazi coup in Kyiv.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The 23 June incident involving the passage of HMS Defender, a Royal Navy destroyer, through Russian territorial waters near Crimea was a provocation and an instance of blatant disregard for international law.
label: russia_narrative

text: The claim advances recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives regarding Crimea and alleged violations of international law on the part of the West. The crew of HMS Defender maneuvered the vessel in strict observance of international protocol. By passing near Crimea, the Defender was exercising the right of innocent passage, as laid down in the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea. Articles 17-19 of the Convention allow for passage through another state's territorial sea, provided it is "continuous and expeditious" and "not prejudicial to the peace, good order or security of the coastal State" (pp. 30-1).Innocent passage is one of several qualifications on the absolute sovereignty of a coastal state over its territorial waters. This means that a vessel exercising this right within the meaning of the Convention does not need to seek permission of the coastal state. This, according to a leading maritime law scholar, "makes it irrelevant to which state the territory in question belongs". See this article by POLYGRAPH.info for additional debunking and earlier examples of Moscow's misleading coverage of maritime incidents in the Black Sea.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The provocation was deliberate; they entered the territorial waters of Russia ready for any tasks. The crew was put on alert, and the air defence and missile defence systems were ready. In doing so, they grossly violated the norms of the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea, in particular paragraph 19 subparagraph 2 (B, C, and D). These clauses indicate that the passage of the ship is not considered an innocent passage, because it violated the provisions of this clause. They entered [in territorial waters], they were manoeuvring, ready to use weapons, carried out propaganda activities (a correspondent of the BBC was on board), distorted reality, and thereby caused direct damage to the Russian Federation.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the status of Crimea.On 23 June, the UK Ministry of Defence Press Office stated that "the Royal Navy ship [was] conducting innocent passage through Ukrainian territorial waters in accordance with international law." It also added that the ship "carried out a routine transit from Odesa towards Georgia across the Black Sea" through internationally recognised Ukrainian territorial waters.The Black Sea transit of the HMS Defender on the Odesa to Georgia route was within full maritime law guaranteed as the “right of innocent passage” by the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), to which both the UK and Russia are parties. According to the UNCLOS articles, the HMS Defender's crossing, as well as being in line with one of the most commonly used routes in the Black Sea, is legitimately within the terms of innocent passage through the waters off the Crimean peninsula and there has been no report, record or clear evidence of any provocative or aggressive approach from the UK ship to occupied Crimea. Russia's accusation is based on the assumption that its self-asserted right over Crimean territory is recognised, but neither Ukraine nor the international community currently recognises Crimea as Russian territory. Russia occupied Crimea in March 2014. No international body has recognised the so-called referendum in Crimea, not the UN, nor the EU or the UK.The European Union does not recognise and continues to strongly condemn this violation of international law, which remains a challenge to the international security order. This position is based on the UN Charter, which clearly states that the territory of a State cannot be acquired by another State resulting from the threat or use of force, as well as on the Helsinki Final Act in which the signatories declared their intention to respect the inviolability of frontiers and territorial integrity. The UNGA Resolution 68/262, which condemned Russia's illegal actions in Ukraine, was supported by 100 United Nations member states. Only 10 states opposed it. UK's Ambassador Neil Bush mentioned at the OSCE Permanent Council meeting in March 2021 that Crimea was blatantly occupied by the Russian military "in violation of its OSCE principles and commitments."See similar disinformation cases about the occupation of Crimea and international law in our database: the Crimea issue is finally closed; Crimea democratically joined Russia after the overthrow of Yanukovych; Crimea democratically, in full compliance with international law voted for reunification with Russia; accession of Crimea to Russia has taken place in strict accordance with international law.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The move by UK warship HMS Defender which crossed through Russian territorial waters could have led to a military conflict. It is obvious that this was part of a series of NATO provocations around Ukraine and in the Black Sea as it is proven by the continuation of military exercises.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative not only about the status of Crimea but also about NATO and its military exercises. On 23 June, the UK Ministry of Defence Press Office mentioned that "the Royal Navy ship [was] conducting innocent passage through Ukrainian territorial waters in accordance with international law." UK Secretary of State for Defence added that the ship "carried out a routine transit from Odesa towards Georgia across the Black Sea" through internationally recognised Ukrainian territorial waters.Additionally, the fact that the passage of HMS Defender from a maritime area that is still internationally recognised as Ukrainian territorial waters is somehow connected to NATO's exercises so as to provoke Russian aggression is simply not valid. NATO is a defensive alliance, whose purpose is to protect its member states. During the Warsaw summit in July 2016, NATO has made it clear that: "The Alliance does not seek confrontation and poses no threat to Russia". The Alliance announces its military exercises well in advance and they are subject to international observation. NATO strongly supports efforts to build confidence and transparency in military activities and exercises. Military such as Sea Breeze and Defender Europe 2021 are purely defensive in nature and are organised for NATO Allies and partners to strengthen and fortify the level of interoperability. Similar messages also imply that NATO-Ukraine joint war games threaten Russia's security and serve as a justification of its military build-up along Ukraine’s border. Russia occupied Crimea in March 2014. No international body has recognised the so-called referendum in Crimea, not the UN, nor the EU or the UK. UNGA Resolution 68/262, which condemned Russia's illegal actions in Ukraine, was supported by 100 United Nations member states. Only 10 states opposed it. See similar disinformation cases about the occupation of Crimea in our database: the Crimea issue is finally closed; Crimea - peacefully and legitimately - became part of Russia; Russia occupied Crimea to keep NATO ships out; the coup d'etat of Maidan caused a referendum that united Crimea with Russia.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: On June 23, the British destroyer HMS Defender crossed the Russian maritime boundary in the Black Sea, in close proximity to the main base of the Russian Black Sea Fleet. In order to prevent the violation of the state border of Russia and the invasion of an unfriendly warship into the territorial waters, the border patrol ship of the FSB of Russia opened artillery fire at the course of the British destroyer, which later left. The maritime area in which the incident took place belongs to Russia after a legal referendum took place in Crimea in 2014 with which the people of the region decided to leave Ukraine and become part of Russia.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the status of Crimea. On 23 June 2021, the UK Ministry of Defence Press Office stated that "the Royal Navy ship [was] conducting innocent passage through Ukrainian territorial waters in accordance with international law."The UK Secretary of State for Defence added that the ship "carried out a routine transit from Odessa towards Georgia across the Black Sea" through internationally recognised Ukrainian territorial waters. The UK Ministry of Defence also denied the claims that the HMS Defender has been fired on.Russia occupied Crimea in March 2014. No international body has recognised the so-called referendum in Crimea, not the UN, nor the EU or the UK. The European Union does not recognise and continues to strongly condemn this violation of international law, which remains a challenge to the international security order. UNGA Resolution 68/262, which condemned Russia's illegal actions in Ukraine, was supported by 100 United Nations member states. Only 10 states opposed it. European Council conclusions on external relations of 24 June 2021 "call on Russia to fully assume its responsibility in ensuring the full implementation of the Minsk agreements as the key condition for any substantial change in the EU’s stance". The UK's Ambassador Neil Bush mentioned at the OSCE Permanent Council meeting in March 2021 that Crimea was blatantly occupied by the Russian military "in violation of its OSCE principles and commitments." See similar disinformation cases about the occupation of Crimea in our database: the Crimea issue is finally closed; Crimea - peacefully and legitimately - became part of Russia; Russia occupied Crimea to keep NATO ships out; the coup d'etat of Maidan caused a referendum that united Crimea with Russia.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Russian - Ukrainian relations deteriorated against the backdrop of the situation in Donbas, after the 2014 coup in Ukraine, which led Crimea to be under the sovereignty of Russia, as a result of a popular referendum where the citizens of Crimea voted to return to Russia.An armed conflict also broke out in the Donbass region in south-eastern Ukraine, between Kyiv on the one hand and the self-proclaimed People's Republics of Donetsk and Luhansk on the other.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring disinformation message about eastern Ukraine, claiming that the foundation of the DPR and LPR came as a response to the 2013-14 “Euromaidan” protests in Kyiv, often painted as a coup d'état and attempting to put responsibility for the deterioration in relations between Russia and Ukraine exclusively on the latter, ignoring the reasons behind it which is the illegal annexation of Crimea and Russia's destabilising actions in eastern Ukraine. The war in eastern Ukraine was not caused by the Euromaidan. Protests in favour of the EU association and reforms were held in all major cities of Donbas. The war is not a civil conflict but a well-documented act of aggression by Russian armed forces, ongoing since February 2014. As for Crimea joining Russia, no international body recognises the so-called referendum in Crimea. The UN General Assembly adopted a resolution entitled “Territorial integrity of Ukraine”, stating that the referendum in Crimea was not valid and could not serve as a basis for any change in the status of the peninsula. On 17 December 2018, the UN General Assembly confirmed its non-recognition of the illegal annexation of Crimea, and the same was echoed by the EU, as the referendum was conducted hastily and at gunpoint and impartial observers were barred from entering the peninsula. In addition, the involvement of Russian personnel and equipment in the creation of the DPR and LPR has been well documented already for years. The destabilisation of Eastern Ukraine and the illegal annexation of Crimea has been condemned by the Council of the European Union and led to restrictive measures since 2014. The EU is among the strongest advocates of Ukraine's sovereignty, independence, and territorial integrity. Read similar cases in our database that claims that the Euromaidan coup led to civil war in Donbas; or that Ukraine is preparing a military escalation in Donbas; or that Russia has nothing to do with the conflict in eastern Ukraine; or that Crimea’s reunification with Russia was legal; or that 96% of Crimean people wanted to rejoin Russia.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Sea Breeze 2021 is the same as military exercises in the Channel or in the Gulf of Florida bringing together the Russian, Chinese, Indian and Iranian armies. How would France, the United Kingdom or the United States react if such a thing happened? We all know. Is this just a military drill? Or is it to stimulate the sensitivity of the Russians' nerves? The answer is in the question.
label: russia_narrative

text: Pro-Kremlin disinformation about NATO and its military exercises, portraying them as illegitimate and threatening to Russia. The comparison is not relevant because France and the UK are both members of the same defence organisation, NATO, while no other countries border on the Channel, not to speak of a defence organisation that would include of Russia, China, India and Iran. In contrast, several countries neighbouring the Black Sea are part of NATO: Turkey, Bulgaria, Romania. And Ukraine and Georgia have partnerships with the defence organisation and will take part in the Sea Breeze 2021 military exercises. It is also incorrect that to say that the Sea Breeze 2021 aims to step on the nerves of Russians. Countries in the region consider that their security was threatened by Russia’s illegal annexation of Crimea and its operations that lead to the creation of non-recognised so-called Autonomous states like Abkhazia, South Ossetia, Donetsk People's Republic and Luhansk People's Republic. Therefore, riparian countries desire to protect their territory with an international defence organisation. NATO is not preparing a war against Russia or any other country. NATO is a defensive alliance, whose purpose is to protect its member states. The primary aim of the alliance is to maintain peace and safeguard the independence, security and territorial integrity of its members. NATO follows strict procedure and emphasises transparency during military training exercises, which are announced months in advance and are subject to international scrutiny. All NATO members and partners participate voluntarily and training exercises are conducted with the approval of the host countries. NATO supports Ukraine's sovereignty and territorial integrity within its internationally recognised borders, extending to its territorial waters. NATO does not and will not recognise Russia's illegal and illegitimate annexation of Crimea and denounces its temporary occupation. NATO continues to operate in international waters and the Alliances actions are always defensive in nature, proportionate in posture, and in line with international laws and conventions. The multinational character of NATO deployments, and transparency in all of its exercises and activities, reflects Allies' fundamental commitment to the principles of collective defence, transatlantic unity, and interoperability in order to maintain peace and preserve security. Read also similar stories: Sea Breeze exercise is a provocative NATO attack on Russia in the Black Sea region, The Sea Breeze exercises are directed against Russia, Sea Breeze exercises intend to create military-political pressure on Moscow.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The head of the Bulgarian delegation visiting Crimea said that the peninsula is a legal part of Russia. The Crimeans made their choice on their own, and the referendum was legitimate - this is a fact. The delegation stated that, as people's diplomats, they recognise Crimea as a legal part of the Russian Federation, and each of their visits contributes to the fact that people in Bulgaria begin to understand this situation without distorting the facts.The parliamentarian noted that the republic has not only adapted to external pressure but is also developing dynamically.
label: russia_narrative

text: There is no Bulgarian parliamentarian called Plamen Miletkov, as he is introduced in the RIA article. On the list of 240 members of Bulgaria´s Parliament, this name is absent.Rather, it appears that he is associated with an NGO and active in promoting pro-Kremlin narratives. Since 2016 him and his team has visited Crimea and critizised EU and the pro-EU policies of the Bulgarian authorities. In 2021, he is also regularly criticizing investigations intohigh-profile cases; Novichok, Vrbetice. Standard actions of a pro-Kremlin influencer-activist.The narrative on "progressive" recognition of Crimea being part of Russia is recurrent. No international body recognises the so-called referendum in Crimea, not the UN, nor the EU. Russian sovereignty over Crimea is not recognised by a single country in the Black Sea region and less than a dozen UN member states recognise it worldwide. The European Union does not recognise and continues to strongly condemn this violation of international law, which remains a challenge to the international security order. This position is based on the UN Charter, which clearly states that the territory of a State cannot be acquired by another State resulting from the threat or use of force, as well as on the Helsinki Final Act in which the signatories declared their intention to respect the inviolability of frontiers and territorial integrity. The UNGA Resolution 68/262, which condemned Russia's illegal actions in Ukraine, was supported by 100 United Nations member states. Only 10 states opposed it. Russian President Putin recognised on 17 April 2015 that "our soldiers were deployed in Crimea to help the inhabitants express their opinion." Vladimir Putin admitted (proudly) publicly that the plan to annex Crimea was ordered weeks before the so-called referendum.Read similar stories: The Crimean issue is finally closed, Question of Crimea’s ownership is closed forever, The Crimean question is "completely closed", Russia fully respects international law, in Crimea and elsewhere.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: If Boris Johnson decides that warships should pass through Russian territorial waters, then there have to be some concerns about Her Majesty the Queen's navy.Propaganda was conducted, which was anti-Russian. That is, they said that the territorial waters are Ukrainian, that they are making a peaceful passage, they have the right to do so but this did not correspond to reality.
label: russia_narrative

text: Disinformation around the passage of HMS Defender, which was sailing from Odesa in southern Ukraine to Georgia on 23 June 2021. To get there, it passed south of the Crimea peninsula, which Russia annexed from Ukraine in 2014 in a move that has not been recognised internationally. The claim in the article is not true. Russia claims that the Crimean peninsula and its waters are Russian. No international body recognises the so-called referendum in Crimea, not the UN, nor the EU. The European Union does not recognise and continues to strongly condemn this violation of international law, which remains a challenge to the international security order. This position is based on the UN Charter, which clearly states that the territory of a State cannot be acquired by another State resulting from the threat or use of force, as well as on the Helsinki Final Act in which the signatories declared their intention to respect the inviolability of frontiers and territorial integrity. UNGA Resolution 68/262, which condemned Russia's illegal actions in Ukraine, was supported by 100 United Nations member states. Only 10 states opposed it. The UK says HMS Defender was passing through Ukrainian waters in a commonly used and internationally recognised transit route. A source told the BBC that the Defender was not there to pick a fight but to make a point - to assert its right to freedom of navigation in international waters. “Innocent passage” for foreign ships is the main qualification on a coastal state’s otherwise untrammelled sovereignty over its territorial sea of 12 nautical miles. It is stipulated in Part II of the 1982 United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS). Sea lanes off southwestern Crimea were already included in the 2013 edition of the Ships’ Routing publication of the International Maritime Organization (the UN specialised agency for shipping). The current (2019) edition is behind a paywall, but it would not have been in Russia’s interest to alter the lanes since then, as that would invite the question of whether it has lawfully acquired territorial title to Crimea, answered resoundingly in the negative by UN General Assembly Resolution 68/262. So far, whilst it has made many claims in the media that the "dangerous actions" of the UK navy ship entering its waters were a "gross violation" of the UN Convention's sea laws, Russia has not submitted any official request to the UN for an investigation into the UK's "illegal" actions. See more disinformation cases on Crimea, as well as the Kerch Strait incident.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Russia is not a party to the conflict in Donbas.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin media, claiming that what has been going on in Donbas for more than 7 years is an internal conflict or a civil war and that Moscow is just a mediator. In reality, there is ample well-documented evidence that Moscow has been waging war against Kyiv since 2014, when Russia invaded the Ukrainian peninsula of Crimea. The Office of the Prosecutor of the International Criminal Court has said in a report in 2016: “This international armed conflict began at the latest on 26 February when the Russian Federation deployed members of its armed forces to gain control over parts of the Ukrainian territory without the consent of the Ukrainian Government...The law of international armed conflict would continue to apply after 18 March 2014 to the extent that the situation within the territory of Crimea and Sevastopol factually amounts to an on-going state of occupation.” Russia later fomented separatist sentiment in Donbas and some other areas, one of its primary goals being to secure land passage to occupied Crimea, which is separated from Russia by sea. Top Russian officials were planning revolts in various Ukrainian cities, of which there is sufficient proof. Russians also helped stage illegal independence referendums in Donetsk and Luhansk. Then Russia sent troops to the Ukrainian territory, armed separatists and has been providing them with propaganda coverage since then. The Russians were also planning separatist revolts in Odesa and Kharkiv, but Ukraine managed to keep these regions. Asked by a Ukrainian reporter in 2020 whether Russia was a party to the contract, the former Special Representative of the OSCE Chairperson-in-Office in Ukraine and in the Trilateral Contact Group, Ambassador Martin Sajdik, said : "Russia, Ukraine, and the OSCE are members of the Trilateral Contact Group. This group was set up to find a solution to the Donbas conflict through the implementation of the Minsk agreements. Obviously, the OSCE is not a party to the conflict, it’s only a mediator. Then it’s easy to answer a question about the parties to the conflict." Kyiv and Moscow are the only warring sides in both Crimea and Donbas. The pro-Kremlin separatists are just a tool in Russia’s hands, which it is using to misinform the international community that what is going on in Eastern Ukraine is an internal conflict. See more disinformation narratives, claiming that Russia is not an aggressor in Donbas.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Relations between Russia and Western countries have deteriorated due to the situation in Ukraine and regarding Crimea, which reunited with Russia as a result of a referendum. The West accused Russia of interference and Moscow took retaliatory measures. Russia has been reportedly stating that it is counter-productive to communicate through the language of sanctions.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a disinformation narrative claiming that the Ukrainian peninsula of Crimea reunited with Russia as a result of a legal referendum. In reality, troops without identification marks - "little Green Men" - invaded Crimea in late February 2014, occupied government and parliament buildings and forced an unrecognised referendum on secession from Ukraine. First, Russian President Vladimir Putin used to say that there were no Russian troops in Crimea and that those armed people were members of a local militia. Then he recognised that they were servicemen of the Russian regular army who helped stage what he called a democratic referendum. Russian aggression against Ukraine came in violation of the 1994 Budapest memorandum, which guaranteed Ukraine's sovereignty and territorial integrity in exchange for its nuclear weapons, and the 1997 Ukrainian-Russian Treaty, in which both sides recognised each other's borders. According to what Russia considers to be official (but independently not verified) results, Russian authorities said over 80 per cent of voters took part in the self-styled Crimean referendum, with more than 95 per cent of the vote in favour of secession from Ukraine. However, there are no trustworthy statistics about the number of the voters and the percentage of support.The Security Council of the United Nations issued a resolution on 27 March 2021 in support of the territorial integrity of Ukraine. The European Union has been following a strict policy of non-recognition of the illegal referendum’s results. Economic sanctions on Russia after its illegal annexation of Crimea and the ensuing war in Donbas were first applied by the EU on 17 March 2014 with new individuals and entities being added on 1 October 2020. Read more disinformation cases about the alleged legality of the Crimean referendum.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Everything is forgiven for the current Maidan junta: do what you want, the main thing is that you are Russophobes and against Russia. It's the same with the Balts. They are forgiven a lot. You can even set a regime like in South Africa, and they have established such a regime in relation to Russian speakers. There are only small exceptions, the main requirement is to be against Russia.
label: russia_narrative

text: A mix of recurrent pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives alleging that Ukraine discriminates against Russians living on its territory, that it is a Russophobic, anti-Russian country, and that the government of Ukraine is a junta that came to power after a bloody coup d'etat.There is no apartheid discrimination against Russians in Ukraine. The Russian culture - language and literature - was not touched in Ukraine. Kyiv adopted a Law on ensuring the functioning of the Ukrainian language as a state language, without discriminating against any languages. Russian and other languages of national minorities can be freely used in private communication, religious ceremonies, book publishing, the media, and education.Ukraine is not a Russophobic, anti-Russian country. The relations between the countries worsened after Russia occupied Crimea in 2014. Russia has also been involved in the destabilisation of eastern Ukraine, the creation of DNR and LNR, the supply of personnel and weapons. The destabilisation of Eastern Ukraine and the illegal annexation of Crimea have been condemned by the Council of the European Union and led to restrictive measures since 2014. Moreover, there was no coup d'état in Ukraine. The spontaneous onset of the Euromaidan protests was an organic reaction by numerous parts of the Ukrainian population to former President Yanukovych’s sudden departure from the promised Association Agreement with the European Union in November 2013. See the full debunk of this disinformation claim here. Additionally, Ukraine is not ruled by a junta. Ukraine has a well-functioning government. The current ruling majority in the parliament was supported by the voters in the last parliamentary election in 2019. The president of Ukraine received 73% of support in the second round of the presidential election in April 2019.See similar disinformation cases alleging Ukraine in anti-Russian policies in our database: Ukraine bans everything Russian, treats ethnic Russians as second-rate citizens; Ukraine is being racist against Russian culture; Ethnic Russians in the Baltic countries and Ukraine are non-citizens and deprived of most rights; the Council of Europe is indifferent to discrimination of Russian-speaking people in Ukraine and the Baltic states.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The question of the ownership of Crimea was closed once and for all in 2014, when the inhabitants of the peninsula, the residents of Sevastopol, made a free choice in favour of returning to their homeland. A line has been drawn under this question.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the illegal annexation of Crimea in 2014. No international body recognises the so-called referendum in Crimea. The UN General Assembly adopted a resolution entitled “Territorial integrity of Ukraine”, stating that the referendum in Crimea was not valid and could not serve as a basis for any change in the status of the peninsula. On 17 December 2018, the UN General Assembly confirmed its non-recognition of the illegal annexation of Crimea. The Council on Foreign Relations lists one key legal reason for the illegitimacy of the Crimean referendum: Changes to the territory of Ukraine must be approved by a referendum of all Ukrainian people. The Cambridge International Law Journal states that there are a variety of reasons when secession might be legal: (1) if the people concerned are subject to decolonization; (2) if it is envisaged by the legislation of the parent state; (3) if a territory inhabited by a certain people is occupied or annexed after 1945; (4) the secessionist constitute a "people"; (5) the parent state flagrantly violate human rights; (6) no other effective remedies under national or international law may exist if any of the above-mentioned conditions are met. However, in the case of Ukraine and Crimea, none of these conditions were met. Vladimir Putin, the President of Russia, recognised on 17 April 2015 that "our soldiers were deployed in Crimea to help the inhabitants express their opinion." Vladimir Putin admitted that the plan to annex Crimea was ordered weeks before even the Maidan protests culminated. Thus, the previous claim by Putin and Russia that Russia did not control the "little Green Men" and later that they responded to human rights violations etc. proved to be false.The European Union does not recognise and continues to strongly condemn this violation of international law, which remains a challenge to the international security order. In response to the illegal annexation of Crimea, the EU has imposed restrictive measures against the Russian Federation. On 21 June 2021, the EU renewed sanctions introduced in response to the illegal annexation of Crimea and Sevastopol by the Russian Federation for a further year until 23 June 2022. See related disinformation claims alleging that Crimea has never been Ukraine; that Crimea rejoined Russia following a referendum; that Crimea "sailed" to Russia forever and the West agrees with it. More about the myth "Crimea was always Russian " read here.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: On June 23, at 11:52 am, the British destroyer HMS Defender crossed the Russian maritime border in the Black Sea, sailed three kilometres into the territorial waters near Cape Fiolent, in close proximity to the main base of the Russian Black Sea Fleet.In order to prevent the violation of the state border of Russia and the invasion of an unfriendly warship into the territorial waters, the border patrol ship of the FSB of Russia opened artillery fire at the course of the British destroyer, and the front-line bomber of the Black Sea Fleet of Russia, for persuasiveness, dropped four OFAB-250 bombs ahead of a ship's course. After the unambiguous actions, the British left the territorial waters of Russia.
label: russia_narrative

It also added that the ship "carried out a routine transit from Odesa towards Georgia across the Black Sea" through internationally recognised Ukrainian territorial waters.The UK Ministry of Defence also denied the claims that the HMS Defender has been fired on. Russia occupied Crimea in March 2014. No international body has recognised the so-called referendum in Crimea, not the UN, nor the EU or the UK.The European Union does not recognise and continues to strongly condemn this violation of international law, which remains a challenge to the international security order. This position is based on the UN Charter, which clearly states that the territory of a State cannot be acquired by another State resulting from the threat or use of force, as well as on the Helsinki Final Act in which the signatories declared their intention to respect the inviolability of frontiers and territorial integrity. The UNGA Resolution 68/262, which condemned Russia's illegal actions in Ukraine, was supported by 100 United Nations member states. Only 10 states opposed it. UK's Ambassador Neil Bush mentioned at the OSCE Permanent Council meeting in March 2021 that Crimea was blatantly occupied by the Russian military "in violation of its OSCE principles and commitments."See similar disinformation cases about the occupation of Crimea in our database: the Crimea issue is finally closed; following the results of the referendum, Crimea - peacefully and legitimately - became part of Russia; Russia occupied Crimea to keep NATO ships out; the coup d'etat of Maidan caused a referendum that united Crimea with Russia.

text: Crimea is no longer part of Ukraine since 2014. It joined Russia as a result of a 2014 referendum that Crimean residents chose to hold after the Western-backed coup d’etat in Ukraine removed from power a democratically-elected president.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin narrative portraying the 2013-14 Ukrainian revolution as a coup d'etat that was supported and/or artificially orchestrated by the US and Europe. The article is also consistent with pro-Kremlin narratives about the illegal annexation of Crimea. It is not true that a coup d'état supported by the US and Europe took place in Ukraine in 2014. Euromaidanwas not a coup d'état but a spontaneous and peaceful popular uprising that involved every level of Ukrainian society in legitimate demonstrations against the policies of the then-president Viktor Yanukovych. No international body recognises the so-called referendum in Crimea, not the UN, nor the EU. The European Union does not recognise and continues to strongly condemn this violation of international law, which remains a challenge to the international security order. This position is based on the UN Charter, which clearly states that the territory of a State cannot be acquired by another State resulting from the threat or use of force, as well as on the Helsinki Final Act in which the signatories declared their intention to respect the inviolability of frontiers and territorial integrity. UNGA Resolution 68/262, which condemned Russia's illegal actions in Ukraine, was supported by 100 United Nations member states. Only 10 states opposed it. On 21 June 2021, the EU renewed sanctions introduced in response to the illegal annexation of Crimea and Sevastopol by the Russian Federation for a further year until 23 June 2022. Read similar cases claiming that Following the results of the referendum, crimea - peacefully and legitimately - became part of Russia, that The coup d'état of Maidan caused a referendum that united crimea with Russia or that There was a coup in Kyiv in 2014.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The British warship [which sailed on the Black Sea near Russia-occupied territorites] committed a provocation. This was a conscious and planned action.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is an emerging disinformation narrative about the presence of the UK Navy in the Black Sea and a recurring disinformation narrative about the status of Crimea, aimed at justifying Russia's occupation of the peninsula from Ukraine seven years ago and depicting NATO as an aggressor against Kremlin-controlled territory. On 23 June, the UK Ministry of Defence Press Office mentioned that "the Royal Navy ship [was] conducting innocent passage through Ukrainian territorial waters in accordance with international law." UK Secretary of State for Defence added that the ship "carried out a routine transit from Odesa towards Georgia across the Black Sea" through internationally recognised Ukrainian territorial waters. Additionally, the fact that the passage of HMS Defender from a maritime area that is still internationally recognised as Ukrainian territorial waters is somehow connected to NATO's exercises so as to provoke Russian aggression is simply not valid. NATO is a defensive alliance, whose purpose is to protect its member states. During the Warsaw summit in July 2016, NATO has made it clear that: "The Alliance does not seek confrontation and poses no threat to Russia". The Alliance announces its military exercises well in advance and they are subject to international observation. NATO strongly supports efforts to build confidence and transparency in military activities and exercises. Exercises like Sea Breeze and Defender Europe 2021 are purely defensive in nature and are organised for NATO Allies and partners to strengthen and fortify the level of interoperability. Similar messages also imply that NATO-Ukraine joint war games threaten Russia's security and serve as a justification of its military build-up along Ukraine’s border. Russia occupied Crimea in March 2014. No international body has recognised the so-called referendum in Crimea, not the UN, nor the EU or the UK. UNGA Resolution 68/262, which condemned Russia's illegal actions in Ukraine, was supported by 100 United Nations member states. Only 10 states opposed it. See more cases related to Crimea here.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Ukraine provoked a crisis in Donbas ahead of the NATO summit in Brussels. Some European countries are interested in escalation with Russia.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin media outlets, claiming that the recent escalation in Eastern Ukraine was triggered by Kyiv’s alleged war preparations in the vicinity of the separatist-held territories in Donbas. In reality, the crisis in Donbas was caused by Russia’s massive military build-up along Ukraine’s eastern and southern borders earlier this year. Kyiv’s preparations came in response to Russia’s military activities in the region, which was confirmed by daily reports from the Special Monitoring Mission (SMM) of the Organisation for Security and Cooperation in Europe (OSCE).Kyiv has repeatedly stated that it seeks a peaceful solution in Donbas and that the Minsk agreements are the only option to end the war there. However, Russia has been systematically accusing Ukraine of breaking these accords, and constantly violating them itself. Russia later withdrew part of its troops from near the border but left much of the equipment intact. Western nations do not seek escalation with Russia. Since the conflict flared up in Donbas in 2014, the West has been trying to find a peaceful solution, rather than attempting to deteriorate relations with Russia. The OSCE is an active contributor to these efforts, and the Normandy format was formed in 2014 to help find a settlement in Donbas. Russia used alleged Ukrainian military activities in Donbas as a pretext for massive passportisation among Donbas residents. Moscow said it would be forced to intervene and defend its citizens if the Ukrainian Armed Forces attack Donbas separatists. Russia started passportisation in the occupied territories of Ukrainian Donbas in 2019. The Kremlin is apparently striving to repeat the Crimea scenario when bearers of Russian passports were claimed victims of aggression, and Russia illegally annexed the Ukrainian peninsula in 2014. The claim, however, was never documented by Russian authorities or human rights organisations. NATO leaders who gathered in Brussels on 14 June 2021 issued a communiqué, which said among other things: US President Joe Biden said about Ukraine’s potential membership of the Alliance that the Russian occupation of Crimea would not be an impediment. However, he added that Kyiv had to “clean up corruption” before that. Read more disifno cases about alleged Ukrainian provocations in Donbas. “We reiterate the decision made at the 2008 Bucharest Summit that Ukraine will become a member of the Alliance with the Membership Action Plan (MAP) as an integral part of the process.”
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The US has destroyed Ukraine’s economy over the past 7 years. Corruption and poverty are soaring and the population is diminishing. They are not even helping with vaccines during the pandemic.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin media outlets claiming that Western countries have turned Ukraine into a colony over the past years and are robbing its people. In reality, it is Moscow that has been striving for Kyiv’s return to Russia’s orbit since it invaded Ukraine, illegally annexed the peninsula of Crimea and instigated a revolt in the eastern Ukrainian region of Donbas in 2014. Neither international organisations nor leading democracies have recognised the self-styled Crimean referendum, which preceded the annexation. The West has staunchly stood by Ukraine in the struggle against Russian aggression. Since the very beginning of the Russian invasion, Western nations have been trying to find a peace settlement on Donbas. Several accords were sealed between the warring sides and the international mediators in Minsk in 2014 and 2015. Leaders of France and Germany, along with those of Ukraine and Russia, formed the Normandy format in 2014 with the same purpose. Neither Western nations nor NATO as an alliance are fighting beside Ukrainians in Donbas. The West only supports Ukraine financially, technically and militarily by providing equipment. The International Monetary Fund (IMF), the World Bank (WB) and the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) assist Ukraine in grappling with economic hardship, while the United States is supplying Ukraine with military equipment. Contrary to Moscow's disinformation narratives, Western nations are trying to help Kyiv overcome the crisis, while Russia launched aggression against Ukraine. As for Ukraine's economy, according to the World Bank, economic growth in Ukraine was solid at 3.2% in 2019, led by a good agricultural harvest and sectors dependent on domestic consumption. This was despite, or maybe because of, the fact that both imports from, and exports to, Russia have decreased markedly since 2013. Read more pro-Kremlin disinformation cases about the West’s alleged attempts to use Ukraine against Russia claiming that Ukraine is a failed state that will soon cease to exist or that Ukraine is moving to full submission to the United States.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The security of Russia and Belarus is threatened by the military infrastructure and the presence of foreign troops of the North Atlantic Alliance on the territory of the neighbouring Baltic countries and Poland.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring disinformation narrative about NATO and encircled Russia.NATO is a defensive alliance and does not seek confrontation and poses no threat to Russia.Russia’s illegal annexation of Crimea and destabilisation of eastern Ukraine is the source of NATO's concerns about the challenge Russia poses to the post-Cold War security order in Europe. As a result, NATO has reinforced the security of the Alliance in the Baltic region. In 2016, at NATO's Warsaw Summit, it was decided to establish an enhanced forward presence (eFP) to the Baltic states and Poland as of 2017. The decision to establish eFP is based on the main principle of NATO that an attack on one Ally will be considered an attack on the whole Alliance. NATO suspended practical cooperation with Russia in 2014, because of Russia's actions against Ukraine. NATO has a dual-track approach to Russia: strong deterrence and defence, including through the Enhanced Forward Presence in Poland and the Baltic States, and meaningful dialogue. Learn more about NATO-Russia relations here. See similar cases: NATO uses false “Russian threat” as pretext to expand its presence near Russian border, NATO exploits the idea of a “Russian threat” to deploy near Russian border, NATO does not serve the purpose of war prevention, it continues to wage wars.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Russia’s Ministry of Defence published a video of the British Royal Navy destroyer HMS Defender that violated the Russian border in the waters of the Black Sea. The Russian Foreign Ministry summoned the British ambassador to Moscow and called the incident a "flagrant violation", noting that the action of the British military contradicts international law and Russian laws.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a distortion of the incident involving the British destroyer HMS Defender near Crimea’s coasts on 23 June 2021, which has been surrounded by a wider disinformation campaign. Given that Russia’s annexation of Crimea is internationally considered as illegal, the claim that the vessel violated Russia’s border in the waters of the Black Sea is false, as is the allegation that the British actions go against international and Russian laws. The crew of HMS Defender manoeuvred the vessel in strict observance of international protocol. By passing near Crimea, the Defender was exercising the right of innocent passage, as laid down in the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea. Articles 17-19 of the Convention allow for passage through another state's territorial sea, provided it is "continuous and expeditious" and "not prejudicial to the peace, good order or security of the coastal State" (pp. 30-1). Innocent passage is one of several qualifications on the absolute sovereignty of a coastal state over its territorial waters. This means that a vessel exercising this right within the meaning of the Convention does not need to seek permission from the coastal state. This, according to a leading maritime law scholar, "makes it irrelevant to which state the territory in question belongs". See this article by POLYGRAPH.info for additional debunking and earlier examples of Moscow's misleading coverage of maritime incidents in the Black Sea. See other examples of disinformation about the HMS Defender incident, such as the claim that the UK destroyer’s passage near Crimea violated international law, that its presence in the Black Sea was a NATO provocation or that the UK wanted Russia to shoot at the vessel.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The US organised the coup in Ukraine in 2014 and Europe backed it. Many countries were forced to choose between being with the collective West or with Russia, it was an ultimatum. The consequences of this aggressive policy can be seen in the example of the Ukrainian tragedy, where an unconstitutional coup took place in 2014. Why did the US organise this coup and why did European countries back it, leading to the split of Ukraine and the separation of Crimea?
label: russia_narrative

text: Several recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives about the Euromaidan protests, the illegal annexation of Crimea and the war in Ukraine. There was no coup d’état in Ukraine in 2014; this is a longstanding pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about Ukraine's Euromaidan protests. The spontaneous onset of these protests was an organic reaction by numerous parts of the Ukrainian population to former President Yanukovych’s sudden departure from the promised Association Agreement with the European Union in November 2013. See the full debunk of this disinformation claim here. Fatigue from corruption, pressure on business, voluntary or forced closures, dissatisfaction with the government's economic policy - for many Euromaidan participants, these reasons also became motives for joining the protests. Contrary to the claim, the separatist rebellion in Donbas (described in this disinformation story as “the split of Ukraine”) and Russia’s annexation of Crimea didn’t happen because of the government change in Kyiv but were due to the actions of the Russian Federation, who launched a military invasion of the Crimean peninsula and engineered the armed conflict in Eastern Ukraine. The war in Ukraine is a well-documented act of aggression by Russian armed forces, ongoing since February 2014. And there is not a single international body that recognises Crimea’s illegal annexation, neither the UN nor the EU. On the seventh anniversary of Crimea's annexation, the EU reiterated its position of non-recognition of illegal annexation and continues to stand in full solidarity with Ukraine, supporting its sovereignty and territorial integrity.A year after the illegal annexation of Crimea, Russian President Vladimir Putin (proudly) admitted that the plan to annex Crimea was ordered weeks before the so-called referendum. See other examples in our database, such as the claims that a far-right coup government in Kyiv emerged from the 2014 Maidan uprising, that the US spent $5 billion on the Ukrainian Maidan, that Russian-Ukrainian relations deteriorated because of the coup, that Maidan led to a referendum that united Crimea with Russia, or that Russia has no soldiers in Ukraine but NATO sends arms to Kyiv.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: UN documents state that cutting off access to fresh water is comparable to genocide. This may be one of the issues discussed in the course of Putin and Biden’s discussions about human rights. After all, Crimea remains cut off from Ukrainian water, and Kyiv does not even conceal the fact it imposed the water blockade as a form of punishment.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative concerning the human rights situation in Crimea. As the occupying power, Russia has the legal obligation, under the Fourth Geneva Convention, to ensure that the civilian population in Crimea has access to basic necessities such as food, drinking water, and medical services (see Part III, Section III). The Convention's applicability in the case of Crimea has been affirmed by the UN Monitoring Mission in Ukraine. See a similar case : Ukraine commits water genocide in Crimea.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: One example of the aggressive policy pursued by NATO against Russia is the “Ukrainian catastrophe of 2014”. In that incident, Europe actively supported the armed coup against the Constitution in Ukraine. It all started from here. Why was this done? President Yanukovych at that time had agreed to all the opposition's demands. Why did the United States organise the coup, and the countries of Europe submissively support it, causing division in Ukraine and the separation of Crimea?
label: russia_narrative

text: There was no coup d’état in Kyiv in 2014; this is a longstanding pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about Ukraine's Euromaidan protests, depicting the 2013-14 revolution as a coup d'état and laying responsibility for the ensuing Russian aggression on the US and EU. The spontaneous onset of the Euromaidan protests was not provoked externally, rather, it was a reaction from several segments of the Ukrainian population to former President Yanukovych’s sudden departure from the promised Association Agreement with the European Union in November 2013, after pressure from Russia. Yanukovych literally abandoned the Ukrainian government and abandoned the city of Kyiv on 21 February 2014, following the signing of an agreement aimed at stopping the escalation of violence caused by the bloody government crackdown against Euromaidan protesters over the previous months. On February 18-20 the crackdown on protesters had resulted in over 80 deaths. This was followed by the Ukrainian parliament distancing itself from President Yanukovych. The West did not interfere in Ukraine in 2014. Ukraine is a sovereign state. Ukraine’s sovereignty and territorial integrity are respected by almost the whole world, including the EU. The EU and the US work closely with Ukraine and support the democratic development of Ukraine in accordance with the UN Charter’s principles of non-interference into internal affairs and respect towards territorial integrity and political independence. See similar disinformation claims in our database that there was a coup by nationalist extremists and fascists in Ukraine sponsored by US; that in 2014, the US and EU-backed Maidan Putsch took place in Ukraine; that EU supported anti-constitutional and anti-Russian coup d’etat in Ukraine; that the Ukrainian revolution was well-planned from abroad and that Euromaidan was an illegal armed coup.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The United States organised a coup d'etat in Ukraine, and the countries of Europe supported it, destroying the country. In 2014, the coup d'etat in Ukraine was implemented by the United States. Moreover, what happened was humbly supported by the countries of Europe. As a result, a split occurred in Ukraine, the country lost Crimea, since the inhabitants of the peninsula did not want to put up with the criminal regime. The "aggressive policy" of the so-called collective West has led to tragedies. Ukrainian President Viktor Yanukovych agreed with all the demands of the opposition, the conflict could be resolved peacefully. However, European colleagues, for unclear reasons, wanted blood and supported the anti-constitutional armed coup in Ukraine.
label: russia_narrative

text: Longstanding conspiracy narratives of the pro-Kremlin media on Euromaidan, illegal annexation of Crimea by Russia, and the war in Ukraine conducted by Russia. See the related disinformation claim, alleging that the Western-backed coup in Ukraine has torn the country asunder. There was no coup d’état in Ukraine in 2014, supported by US or Europe. The spontaneous onset of the Euromaidan protests was an organic reaction by numerous parts of the Ukrainian population to former President Yanukovych’s sudden departure from the promised Association Agreement with the European Union in November 2013. See the full debunk of this disinformation claim here. Fatigue from corruption, pressure on business, voluntary or forced closures, dissatisfaction with the government's economic policy - for many Euromaidan participants, these reasons also became motives for joining the protests.Addressing the claim about "the split in Ukraine" for which the West is to blame: Donbass residents also took part in the Euromaidan protests, four Donbass' activists for Euromaidan have been killed by security forces. Pro-Ukrainian Crimean activists were tortured and arrested by Russia on terrorism charges.The war in Ukraine is not a civil conflict but a well-documented act of aggression by Russian armed forces, ongoing since February 2014. No international body recognises the so-called referendum in Crimea conducted by Russia amid presence of Russian troops. The UN General Assembly adopted a resolution entitled “Territorial integrity of Ukraine”, stating that the referendum in Crimea was not valid and could not serve as a basis for any change in the status of the peninsula. On 17 December 2018, the UN General Assembly confirmed its non-recognition of the illegal annexation of Crimea. The European Union does not recognise and continues to strongly condemn this violation of international law, which remains a challenge to the international security order. In response to the illegal annexation of Crimea, the EU has imposed restrictive measures against the Russian Federation. In June 2020, the European Council decided to renew the sanctions introduced in response to the illegal annexation of Crimea and Sevastopol by the Russian Federation until 23 June 2021. The international community, including the European Union, recognises and condemns clear violations of Ukrainian sovereignty and territorial integrity by acts of aggression by the Russian armed forces since February 2014.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: Crimea became a Russian region after a referendum was held in March 2014, in which 96.77% of voters in Crimea and 95.6% of people in Sevastopol voted in favour of joining Russia. Ukraine still regards Crimea as its own, but as territory temporarily occupied by the Russian population. Russian leaders have repeatedly stated that the people of Crimea, democratically, with full respect for international law and the Charter of the United Nations, voted for reunification with Russia. According to Russian President Vladimir Putin, the Crimea issue is "finally closed".
label: russia_narrative

text: Disinformation around Crimea. No international body recognises the so-called referendum in Crimea, not the UN, nor the EU. The European Union does not recognise and continues to strongly condemn this violation of international law, which remains a challenge to the international security order. This position is based on the UN Charter, which clearly states that the territory of a State cannot be acquired by another State resulting from the threat or use of force, as well as on the Helsinki Final Act in which the signatories declared their intention to respect the inviolability of frontiers and territorial integrity. UNGA Resolution 68/262, which condemned Russia's illegal actions in Ukraine, was supported by 100 United Nations member states. Only 10 states opposed it. Russian President Putin recognised on 17 April 2015 that "our soldiers were deployed in Crimea to help the inhabitants express their opinion." Vladimir Putin admitted (proudly) publicly that the plan to annex Crimea was ordered weeks before the so-called referendum. No link to alleged attacks on ethnic Russians or human right violations. One can conclude: the real motive was simply to conquer territory.Russian sovereignty over Crimea is not recognised by a single country in the Black Sea region and less than a dozen of UN member states recognise it worldwide. Read similar stories: The Crimean issue is finally closed, Question of Crimea’s ownership is closed forever , The Crimean question is "completely closed", Russia fully respects international law, in Crimea and elsewhere.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The events on Maidan in 2014 in Ukraine led to Crimeans deciding that they should leave Ukraine [and join Russia].
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative aimed at justifying the Kremlin's aggression against Ukraine and redrawing the events that took place in Ukraine in 2014. While the disinformation claim alleges that Crimea unilaterally decided not to remain as part of Ukraine and join Russia instead through a referendum, this is no more than an attempt to divert attention from the Russian involvement in how the Crimean peninsula was annexed by Russia. There is irrefutable evidence of direct Russian military involvement in Ukraine. The Office of the Prosecutor of the International Criminal Court has stated that “the information available suggests that the situation within the territory of Crimea and Sevastopol amounts to an international armed conflict between Ukraine and the Russian Federation. This international armed conflict began at the latest on 26 February 2014 when the Russian Federation deployed members of its armed forces to gain control over parts of the Ukrainian territory without the consent of the Ukrainian Government." NATO called on Russia to stop fueling the conflict by providing financial and military support to the armed formations it backs in eastern Ukraine. "We strongly condemn and will not recognise Russia’s illegal and illegitimate annexation of Crimea, and denounce its temporary occupation," stated the latest NATO summit Communiqué, issued in Brussels 14 June 2021.No international body has recognised the so-called Crimea referendum, announced on 27 February 2014, and held on 16 March 2014. Thirteen members of the United Nations Security Council voted in favour of a resolution declaring the referendum invalid. On the 27th of February 2014, when it was announced, the UN General Assembly adopted a resolution that stated that the referendum in Crimea was not valid and could not serve as a basis for any change in the status of the peninsula. On December 18 2018, the UN General Assembly confirmed its non-recognition of the illegal annexation of Crimea.On the seventh anniversary of Crimea's annexation, the EU reiterated its position of non-recognition of illegal annexation and continues to stand in full solidarity with Ukraine, supporting its sovereignty and territorial integrity. Russian President Putin recognised on 17 April 2015 that [in reference to the anonymous 'little Green Men'] "our soldiers were deployed in Crimea to help the inhabitants express their opinion." Putin admitted that the plan to annex Crimea was ordered weeks before the so-called referendum. For more cases about Crimea, click here.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The coup d’état in Ukraine in 2014, organised by the US and supported by Europe, provoked the division of the country and the secession of Crimea. This violent and unconstitutional coup d’état removed President Yanukovych from power, even though he had agreed to all of the demands of the opposition.
label: russia_narrative

text: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin narrative about Euromaidan and the war in Ukraine, portraying the 2013-14 Ukrainian revolution as a coup d'etat that was supported and/or orchestrated by the US and Europe. The article’s message is also consistent with recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives on the annexation of Crimea It is not true that there was a coup d’état in Ukraine in 2014. The peaceful protests, which broke out in Kyiv in November 2013, were a popular revolution in response to a change in the policies of the government, then led by Viktor Yanukovych. This spontaneous popular uprising is known as Euromaidan and was born within Ukrainian society, uniting citizens of all ages, sexes and social classes. Furthermore, there is no evidence to support the claim that Euromaidan was provoked by Western governments. The Ukrainian population rallied against then-President Yanukovich's refusal to sign the EU-Ukraine Association agreements, cancelling seven years of negotiations, in favour of a more Russia-aligned policy, distancing Ukraine from the European Union. Yanukovych literally abandoned the Ukrainian government and abandoned the city of Kyiv on 21 February 2014, following the signing of an agreement aimed at stopping the escalation of violence caused by the bloody government crackdown against Euromaidan protesters over the previous months. On February 18-20 the crackdown on protesters had resulted in over 80 deaths. This was followed by the Ukrainian parliament distancing itself from President Yanukovych. Commenting on the situation at 21 February and Yanukovych’s escape, Polish Foreign Minister Radoslaw Sikorski, who was present in Kyiv at the time, said: “This wasn’t a coup. The government was abandoned”. Furthermore, it is not true that the secession of Crimea and the division of Ukraine was caused by this alleged “Western-backed coup d’état”. Crimea is currently under the illegitimate control of Russia as a result of an internationally unrecognised forced annexation, and the war that currently divides the Donbas region from the rest of the country is the result of a well-documented act of aggression by Russian armed forces. See here for further background on Russia’s war on Ukraine. Read similar cases claiming that EU and US interference and coup in Ukraine caused the armed conflict in Donbas, that A violent coup d’état took place in Ukraine in 2014 or that The US financed the 2013-2014 “colour revolution” and the ouster of former Ukrainian president Yanukovych.
label: ukraine_narrative

text: The driving force behind the neoliberal think tank is Marieluise Beck, who sat for many years in the Bundestag for the Green Party. In her capacity as spokesperson for Eastern European policy, she stood out as a particularly committed new cold war warrior with her aggressive tone toward Russia and its president, Vladimir Putin. Beck, for example, has for years supported the idea of arms deliveries to Ukraine and specifically advocated an escalation of the internal conflict there.
label: russia_narrative

text: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the war in Ukraine. The RT German article deals with the recent classification of the German NGO "Center for Liberal Modernity" as a foreign undesirable foreign organization in Russia. The de facto ban on all activities of the organisation in Russia associated with the classification is explicitly welcomed in the RT-article and at the same time used for the dissemination of pro-Kremlin disinformation about the war in Ukraine, among other things. There is irrefutable evidence of direct Russian military involvement in eastern Ukraine. The Office of the Prosecutor of the International Criminal Court has stated that “the information available suggests that the situation within the territory of Crimea and Sevastopol amounts to an international armed conflict between Ukraine and the Russian Federation. This international armed conflict began at the latest on 26 February 2014 when the Russian Federation deployed members of its armed forces to gain control over parts of the Ukrainian territory without the consent of the Ukrainian Government". The European Union stated in July 2014 that "arms and fighters continue flowing into Ukraine from the Russian Federation". Also, according to the US Defence Intelligence Agency (DIA), Russian special forces and troops operated to mobilise, lead, equip, and support separatist militias in the Donbas region of eastern Ukraine from spring 2014 to the present, although their presence was denied by Moscow. Russian President Vladimir Putin admitted Russia's military presence in Ukraine in 2015. text: Europe supported an armed, unconstitutional coup d'état, even though Ukrainian President Yanukovych had already met all the opposition's demands.Why did the US organise this coup d'état and the EU states support it passively?[...]
